Subsection.Text: In any action or proceeding respecting a patent, a written communication, or any part of such a communication, may be admitted into evidence to rebut any representation made by the patentee in the action or proceeding as to the construction of a claim in the patent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is prepared in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the prosecution of the application for the patent,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a disclaimer made in respect of the patent, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a request for re-examination, or a re-examination proceeding, in respect of the patent; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is between

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the applicant for the patent or the patentee; and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Commissioner, an officer or employee of the Patent Office or a member of a re-examination board.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, the prosecution of a divisional application is deemed to include the prosecution of the original application before that divisional application is filed.
For the purposes of this section, a written communication is deemed to be prepared in respect of the prosecution of the application for a reissued patent if it is prepared in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the prosecution of the application for the patent that was surrendered and from which the reissued patent results; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the application for reissuance.

Section.Text: Subsection 55.2(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not affect any exception to the exclusive property or privilege granted by a patent that exists at law in respect of acts done privately and on a non-commercial scale or for a non-commercial purpose.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 55.2:

Subsection.Text: An act committed for the purpose of experimentation relating to the subject-matter of a patent is not an infringement of the patent.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: factors that the court may consider, must consider or is not permitted to consider in determining whether an act is, or is not, committed for the purpose set out in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: circumstances in which an act is, or is not, committed for the purpose set out in subsection (1).

Section.Text: Section 56 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), if — before the claim date of a claim in a patent — a person, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of the patent in respect of that claim, or made serious and effective preparations to commit such an act, it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent, in respect of that claim, if the person commits the same act on or after that claim date.

If the act referred to in subsection (1) is committed or the preparations to commit it are made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the act was committed or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (1) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply to an act committed by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent, in respect of the claim, if the transferee commits the act after the transfer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception — use or sale of article
Subsection.Text: The use or sale of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if that article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, at the time they disposed of it, could sell it without infringing the patent or the certificate

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: because the person, before the claim date of a claim in the patent, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of the patent in respect of that claim and they disposed of the article before that claim date; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under subsection (1) or paragraph (2)(b).

Subsection.Text: The use of a service is not an infringement of a patent if the service is provided by a person who, under subsection (1) or paragraph (2)(b), is able to provide it without infringing the patent.

Subsection (1) or paragraph (3)(a) does not apply if the person referred to in that subsection or that paragraph was able, as the case may be, to commit the act or make the preparations to commit the act only because they obtained knowledge of the subject-matter defined by the claim, directly or indirectly, from the applicant of the application on the basis of which the patent was granted and they knew that the applicant was the source of the knowledge.

Subject to subsection (7), the use of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent, in respect of a claim, if the article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, before the claim date of that claim, in good faith, made or sold, or made serious and effective preparations to make or sell, an article that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If the making or selling referred to in subsection (6) was done or the preparations to do so were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the making or selling was done or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (6) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply in respect of an article that is made or sold by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent, in respect of a claim referred to in subsection (6), to use an article for the use referred to in that subsection if it was made or sold for that use by the transferee after the transfer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (6) does not apply if the person referred to in that subsection was able to make or sell, or to make the preparations to make or sell, the article only because they obtained knowledge of the use defined by the claim, directly or indirectly, from the applicant of the application on the basis of which the patent was granted and they knew that the applicant was the source of the knowledge.

Subject to subsection (10), the use of a service is not an infringement of a patent in respect of a claim if the service is provided by a person who, before the claim date of that claim, in good faith, provided, or made serious and effective preparations to provide, a service that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If the service referred to in subsection (9) was provided or the preparations to provide it were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the service was provided or the preparations to do so were made, is subsequently transferred, then, after the transfer

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to no longer be the person referred to in subsection (9) for the purposes of that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to be the person who provided the service for the purposes of subsection (9).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (9) does not apply if the person referred to in that subsection was able to provide the service or make the preparations to provide it only because they obtained knowledge of the use defined by the claim, directly or indirectly, from the applicant of the application on the basis of which the patent was granted and they knew that the applicant was the source of the knowledge.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 76.1:

Subsection.Text: Any written demand received by a person in Canada, that relates to an invention that is patented in Canada or elsewhere or that is protected by a certificate of supplementary protection in Canada or by analogous rights granted elsewhere, must comply with the prescribed requirements.

Any person who receives a written demand that does not comply with the prescribed requirements, and any person who is aggrieved as a result of the receipt by another person of such a written demand, may bring a proceeding in the Federal Court.

If the Federal Court is satisfied that the written demand does not comply with the prescribed requirements, it may grant any relief that it considers appropriate, including by way of recovery of damages, punitive damages, an injunction, a declaration or an award of costs.

If a corporation sends a written demand that does not comply with the prescribed requirements, is notified of those requirements and of the demand’s defects in respect of those requirements and does not, within a reasonable time after receiving the notice of those defects, remedy them, the corporation’s officers, directors, agents or mandataries are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the corporation if they directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the sending of the demand.
A person is not to be found liable under subsection (4) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to ensure that the written demand complies with the prescribed requirements.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of section 76.2, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting what constitutes a written demand or an aggrievement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the requirements with which a written demand must comply;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting factors that the Federal Court may consider, must consider or is not permitted to consider in making an order under subsection 76.2(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which a defendant is not to be found liable in a proceeding brought under subsection 76.2(2).

Section.Text: Section 78.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (3), any matter arising on or after the day on which this subsection comes into force in respect of a patent issued on the basis of an application whose filing date is before October 1, 1989 shall be dealt with and disposed of in accordance with sections 38.1, 45, 46 and 48.1 to 48.5 and with the provisions of this Act, other than sections 46 and 56, as they read immediately before October 1, 1989.

The provisions of this Act that apply as provided in subsection (1) shall be read subject to any amendments to this Act, other than the amendments that came into force on October 1, 1989 or October 1, 1996 or that were made by sections 188, 189, and 195 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No 2.

Section 56 of the Patent Act, as it read immediately before October 1, 1989, applies in respect of a purchase, construction or acquisition made before October 1, 1996 of an invention for which a patent is issued on the basis of an application filed before October 1, 1989.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 123:

Heading.TitleText: Legal Proceedings in Respect of Certificates of Supplementary Protection

Section.Text: A written communication, or any part of such a communication, that is admissible under section 53.1 in respect of a patent set out in a certificate of supplementary protection may be admitted into evidence to any action or proceeding respecting the certificate of supplementary protection to rebut any representation made by the holder of the certificate of supplementary protection in the action or proceeding as to the construction of a claim in the patent set out in the certificate of supplementary protection.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 124(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 55.3(1), with any reference to “patent” to be read as a reference to “certificate of supplementary protection”;

Subsection.Text: Section 124 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: factors that the court may consider, must consider or is not permitted to consider in determining whether an act is, or is not, committed for the purpose of experimentation relating to the subject-matter of the certificate of supplementary protection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: circumstances in which an act is, or is not, committed for the purpose of experimentation relating to the subject-matter of the certificate of supplementary protection.

Section.Text: Paragraph 134(1)(f) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which any patentee or holder of a certificate of supplementary protection may or must be represented by another person in respect of a certificate of supplementary protection or an application for such a certificate;

Section.Text: Section 52.1 of the Patent Act applies in respect of any action or proceeding that has not been finally disposed of on the coming into force of that section.
Sections 53.1 and 123.1 of the Patent Act apply in respect of any action or proceeding that has not been finally disposed of on the coming into force of that section 53.1.
Section 55.3 and paragraph 124(1)(f) of the Patent Act apply in respect of any action or proceeding that has not been finally disposed of on the coming into force of that section 55.3.

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Patent Act, as enacted by section 194 of this Act, applies only in respect of an action or proceeding in respect of a patent issued on the basis of an application whose filing date is on or after October 1, 1989 that is commenced on or after October 29, 2018.

Section 56 of the Patent Act, as it read immediately before the coming into force of section 194 of this Act, applies in respect of any action or proceeding that is in respect of a patent issued on the basis of an application whose filing date is on or after October 1, 1989 and that is commenced before October 29, 2018.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments to the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2

Section.Text: Subsection 118(5) of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2 is amended by replacing the paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.75)
Paragraph.Text: establishing a period for the purposes of subsections 55.11(3), (7) and (9);

Section.Text: Section 119 of the Act is amended by repealing the section 15.1 that it enacts.
Subsection 125(1) of the Act is amended by replacing the subsection 28.4(2.1) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: Except for the purposes of subsection 10(3), a request for priority is deemed never to have been made if the request is not made in accordance with the regulations or if the applicant does not submit the information, other than the number of each previously regularly filed application, required under subsection (2).
Section 136 of the Act is amended by replacing the portion of the subsection 55.11(1) before paragraph (a) that it enacts with the following:
This section applies only in respect of the following patents and certificates of supplementary protection that set out the following patents:
Section 136 of the Act is amended by replacing the subsections 55.11(2) to (4) that it enacts with the following:

If, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.74) that relates to a patent, a person, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of that patent, that act is not an infringement of the patent.

Subject to subsection (4), if — during a period established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to a patent — a person, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of that patent or made serious and effective preparations to commit that act, it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the person commits the act after that period.

If the act referred to in subsection (3) is committed or the preparations to commit it are made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the act was committed or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (3) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply in respect of an act committed by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the transferee commits the act after the transfer.

Subsection.Text: The use or sale of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if that article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, at the time they disposed of it, could, under subsection (2) or (3) or paragraph (4)(b), sell the article without infringing the patent or the certificate.

The use of a service is not an infringement of a patent if the service is provided by a person who, under subsection (2) or (3) or paragraph (4)(b), is able to provide it without infringing the patent.

Subject to subsection (8), the use of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to that patent, in good faith, made or sold, or made serious and effective preparations to make or sell, an article that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If the making or selling referred to in subsection (7) was done or the preparations to do so were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the making or selling was done or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (7) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply in respect of an article that is made or sold by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of a patent or a certificate of supplemental protection referred to in subsection (7) to use an article for the use referred to in that subsection if it was made or sold for that use by the transferee after the transfer.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (10), the use of a service is not an infringement of a patent if the service is provided by a person who, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to that patent, provided, or made serious and effective preparations to provide, a service that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If, during the period referred to in subsection (9), the service was provided or the preparations to provide it were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the service was provided or the preparations to do so were made, is subsequently transferred, then, after the transfer

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to no longer be the person referred to in subsection (9) for the purposes of that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to be the person who provided the service for the purposes of subsection (9).

Section.Text: Section 140 of the Act is amended by replacing the section 78.53 that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection 78.55(2), any matter arising on or after the coming-into-force date, in respect of a patent granted on the basis of an application whose filing date is before October 1, 1989, shall be dealt with and disposed of in accordance with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act, other than the definitions claim date, filing date and request for priority in section 2, sections 10, 27 to 28.4, 34.1 to 36, 38.2 and 55, paragraphs 55.11(1)(a) and (b) and section 56; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 10 and 55 and subsections 61(1) and (3), as they read immediately before October 1, 1989.

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Patent Act, as it read immediately before October 1, 1989, applies in respect of a purchase, construction or acquisition made before October 1, 1996 of an invention for which a patent is issued on the basis of an application filed before October 1, 1989.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Canada–European Union Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement Implementation Act

Section.Text: Subsection 135(11) of the Canada–European Union Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement Implementation Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2.
If section 139 of the other Act comes into force before section 196 of this Act, then that section 196 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
If section 139 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 196 of this Act, then that section 196 is deemed to have come into force before that section 139.
If subsection 118(5) of the other Act comes into force before section 204 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 204 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 12(1)(j.75) of the Patent Act is replaced with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.75)
Paragraph.Text: establishing a period for the purposes of subsections 55.11(3), (7) and (9);

Subsection.Text: If subsection 118(5) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 204 of this Act, then that section 204 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 118(5).
If section 119 of the other Act comes into force before section 205 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 205 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 15.1 of the Patent Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: If section 119 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 205 of this Act, then that section 205 is deemed to have come into force before that section 119.
If subsection 125(1) of the other Act comes into force before section 206 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 206 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 28.4(2.1) of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Except for the purposes of subsection 10(3), a request for priority is deemed never to have been made if the request is not made in accordance with the regulations or if the applicant does not submit the information, other than the number of each previously regularly filed application, required under subsection (2).
If subsection 125(1) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 206 of this Act, then that section 206 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 125(1).
If section 136 of the other Act comes into force before section 207 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 207 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 55.11(1) of the Patent Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This section applies only in respect of the following patents and certificates of supplementary protection that set out the following patents:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 55.11(2) to (10) of the Patent Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.74) that relates to a patent, a person, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of that patent, that act is not an infringement of the patent.

Subject to subsection (4), if — during a period established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to a patent — a person, in good faith, committed an act that would otherwise constitute an infringement of that patent or made serious and effective preparations to commit that act, it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the person commits the act after that period.

If the act referred to in subsection (3) is committed or the preparations to commit it are made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the act was committed or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (3) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply in respect of an act committed by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of the patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the transferee commits the act after the transfer.

Subsection.Text: The use or sale of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if that article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, at the time they disposed of it, could, under subsection (2) or (3) or paragraph (4)(b), sell the article without infringing the patent or the certificate.

The use of a service is not an infringement of a patent if the service is provided by a person who, under subsection (2) or (3) or paragraph (4)(b), is able to provide it without infringing the patent.

Subject to subsection (8), the use of an article is not an infringement of a patent or any certificate of supplementary protection that sets out the patent if the article was acquired, directly or indirectly, from a person who, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to that patent, in good faith, made or sold, or made serious and effective preparations to make or sell, an article that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If the making or selling referred to in subsection (7) was done or the preparations to do so were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the making or selling was done or the preparations were made, is subsequently transferred, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (7) or paragraph (b), as the case may be, does not apply in respect of an article that is made or sold by the transferor after the transfer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is not an infringement of a patent or a certificate of supplemental protection referred to in subsection (7) to use an article for the use referred to in that subsection if it was made or sold for that use by the transferee after the transfer.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (10), the use of a service is not an infringement of a patent if the service is provided by a person who, during a period that is established by regulations made under paragraph 12(1)(j.75) that relates to that patent, provided, or made serious and effective preparations to provide, a service that is substantially the same as the one used, for that use.

If, during the period referred to in subsection (9), the service was provided or the preparations to provide it were made in the course of a business and that business, or the part of that business in the course of which the service was provided or the preparations to do so were made, is subsequently transferred, then, after the transfer

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to no longer be the person referred to in subsection (9) for the purposes of that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is deemed to be the person who provided the service for the purposes of subsection (9).

Subsection.Text: If section 136 of the other Act and section 207 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 207 is deemed to have come into force before that section 136.
If section 140 of the other Act comes into force before section 208 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 208 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 78.53 of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection 78.55(2), any matter arising on or after the coming-into-force date, in respect of a patent granted on the basis of an application whose filing date is before October 1, 1989, shall be dealt with and disposed of in accordance with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act, other than the definitions claim date, filing date and request for priority in section 2, sections 10, 27 to 28.4, 34.1 to 36, 38.2 and 55, paragraphs 55.11(1)(a) and (b) and section 56; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 10 and 55 and subsections 61(1) and (3), as they read immediately before October 1, 1989.

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Patent Act, as it read immediately before October 1, 1989, applies in respect of a purchase, construction or acquisition made before October 1, 1996, of an invention for which a patent is issued on the basis of an application filed before October 1, 1989.
If section 140 of the other Act and section 208 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 208 is deemed to have come into force before that section 140.
In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1.
If section 65 of the other Act comes into force before section 189 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 189 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 36(4) of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A divisional application shall be deemed to be a separate and distinct application under this Act, to which its provisions apply as fully as may be, and separate fees shall be paid on the divisional application and, except for the purposes of subsections 27(6) and (7), it shall have the same filing date as the original application.
If section 65 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 189 of this Act, then that section 189 is deemed to have come into force before that section 65.
On the first day on which both section 65 of the other Act and section 191 of this Act are in force, subsection 53.1(2) of the French version of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:
Pour l’application du présent article, la poursuite de toute demande divisionnaire est réputée comprendre la poursuite de la demande originale avant le dépôt de cette demande divisionnaire.
In this section, other Act means the Canada–European Union Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement Implementation Act.
If subsection 135(11) of the other Act produces its effects before section 209 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 209 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 124(1)(d.1) of the Patent Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 135(11) of the other Act produces its effects on the day on which section 209 of this Act comes into force, then that subsection 135(11) is deemed to have produced its effects before that section 209 comes into force.

Section 189 and subsection 198(2) come into force on the day on which subsection 120(2) and section 136 of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2 come into force or, if it is later, on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Section 199 is deemed to have come into force on September 21, 2017.

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition pays d’origine in section 2 of the French version of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: Le pays de l’Union où l’auteur d’une demande d’enregistrement d’une marque de commerce avait, à la date de la demande, un établissement industriel ou commercial effectif et sérieux;

Section.Text: Section 9 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, and despite any public notice of adoption and use given by the Registrar under paragraph (1)(n), subparagraph (1)(n)(iii) does not apply with respect to a badge, crest, emblem or mark if the entity that made the request for the public notice is not a public authority or no longer exists.

In the circumstances set out in subsection (3), the Registrar may, on his or her own initiative or at the request of a person who pays a prescribed fee, give public notice that subparagraph (1)(n)(iii) does not apply with respect to the badge, crest, emblem or mark.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Despite section 11, a person may use a badge, crest, emblem or mark described in subparagraph 9(1)(n)(iii) if, at the time of use, the entity that made the request for a public notice under paragraph 9(1)(n) with respect to the badge, crest, emblem or mark is not a public authority or no longer exists.

Subsection.Text: Section 11.13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6.1):

If, in the opinion of the Registrar, an objector is in default in the continuation of an objection, the Registrar may, after giving notice to the objector of the default, treat the objection as withdrawn unless the default is remedied within the time specified in the notice.
Section 11.13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):
Subject to the regulations, the Registrar may, by order, award costs in a proceeding under this section.
A certified copy of an order made under subsection (9) may be filed in the Federal Court and, on being filed, the order becomes and may be enforced as an order of that Court.

Section.Text: Subsection 18(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the application for registration was filed in bad faith.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 36:

If, in the opinion of the Registrar, an opponent is in default in the continuation of an opposition referred to in section 38, the Registrar may, after giving notice to the opponent of the default, treat the opposition as withdrawn unless the default is remedied within the time specified in the notice.
Subsection 38(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: that the application was filed in bad faith;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 38:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, the Registrar may, by order, award costs in a proceeding under section 38.
A certified copy of an order made under subsection (1) may be filed in the Federal Court and, on being filed, the order becomes and may be enforced as an order of that Court.

Section.Text: Section 45 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, the Registrar may, by order, award costs in a proceeding under this section.
A certified copy of an order made under subsection (4.1) may be filed in the Federal Court and, on being filed, the order becomes and may be enforced as an order of that Court.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 45:

Subsection.Text: A party to a proceeding under section 11.13, 38 or 45 may make a request to the Registrar, in accordance with the regulations, that some or all of the evidence that they intend to submit to the Registrar be kept confidential.

The Registrar shall not consider a request if the party who makes it submits the evidence to the Registrar before the Registrar either gives notice under subsection (3) or makes an order under subsection (4).
If the Registrar is not satisfied that the evidence should be kept confidential, the Registrar shall notify the party who made the request accordingly.
If the Registrar is satisfied that the evidence should be kept confidential, he or she may, on any terms that he or she considers appropriate, order that the evidence be kept confidential.
If the Registrar makes an order under subsection (4),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the party who made the request may submit the evidence to the Registrar and, if it is submitted, shall serve it on the other party in accordance with the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 11.13(5.1), 38(9) and 45(2.1) do not apply with respect to the evidence;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 29 does not apply to the evidence; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Registrar shall take measures to ensure that the evidence is kept confidential when the evidence or a copy of the evidence is transmitted to the Federal Court under section 60.

Subsection.Text: A certified copy of an order made under subsection (4) may be filed in the Federal Court and, on being filed, the order becomes and may be enforced as an order of that Court.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 46:
The registration of a trademark may be renewed for any of the goods or services in respect of which the trademark is registered.
Section 53.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: If, within a period of three years beginning on the date of registration of a trademark, the owner of the registered trademark makes an application claiming that an act has been done contrary to section 19, 20 or 22, the owner is not entitled to relief unless the trademark was in use in Canada at any time during that period or special circumstances exist that excuse the absence of use in Canada during that period.

Section.Text: Subsection 56(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, on an appeal under subsection (1), the Federal Court grants leave to adduce evidence in addition to that adduced before the Registrar, the Court may exercise, with respect to that additional evidence, any discretion vested in the Registrar.

Section.Text: Section 65 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (m) and by replacing paragraph (n) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the awarding of costs under subsections 11.13(9), 38.1(1) and 45(4.1);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: respecting requests made under subsection 45.1(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that by this Act is to be prescribed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 65.2:

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the Governor in Council may make regulations under paragraphs 65(i) and 65.2(b) respecting the case management by the Registrar of proceedings referred to in those paragraphs.

The Governor in Council may make regulations authorizing the Registrar to fix, despite any time or manner that is provided for under this Act with respect to any of the proceedings referred to in subsection (1), the time by which or the manner in which any step in a case-managed proceeding is to be completed and, in that case, the provisions of this Act and the regulations apply with respect to the proceeding with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 68.1:

No person shall oppose an application for registration of a trademark on the ground set out in paragraph 38(2)(a.1) if the application was advertised under subsection 37(1) before the day on which that paragraph comes into force.
Subsection 70(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a regulation made under this Act applies to an application referred to in subsection (1), unless the regulation provides otherwise.

Section.Text: Subsection 326(4) of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 is amended by replacing the subsection 12(3) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: A trademark that is not registrable by reason of paragraph (1)(a) or (b) is registrable if it is distinctive at the filing date of an application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), having regard to all the circumstances of the case including the length of time during which it has been used.

Section.Text: Section 339 of the Act is amended by replacing the portion of subsection 32(1) before paragraph (a) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant shall furnish the Registrar with any evidence that the Registrar may require establishing that the trademark is distinctive at the filing date of the application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), if any of the following apply:

Section.Text: Subsection 343(2) of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 38(2)(e) and (f) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that, at the filing date of the application in Canada, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), the applicant was not using and did not propose to use the trademark in Canada in association with the goods or services specified in the application; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that, at the filing date of the application in Canada, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), the applicant was not entitled to use the trademark in Canada in association with those goods or services.

Section.Text: Section 344 of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 39(1)(a) and (b) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Section.Text: Subsection 367(60) of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 39(1)(a) and (b) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Section.Text: Subsection 15(4) of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act is amended by replacing the subsection 12(3) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: A trade-mark that is not registrable by reason of paragraph (1)(a) or (b) is registrable if it is distinctive at the filing date of an application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), having regard to all the circumstances of the case including the length of time during which it has been used.

Section.Text: Section 31 of the Act is amended by replacing the portion of subsection 32(1) before paragraph (a) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant shall furnish the Registrar with any evidence that the Registrar may require establishing that the trade-mark is distinctive at the filing date of the application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), if any of the following apply:

Section.Text: Section 36 of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 39.1(1)(a) and (b) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1.
If section 230 of this Act comes into force before subsection 69(2) of the other Act, then that subsection 69(2) is repealed.
If section 230 of this Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 69(2) of the other Act, then that subsection 69(2) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

On the first day on which both subsection 70(7) of the other Act has produced its effects and section 215 of this Act is in force, subsection 70(1) of the Trade-marks Act is amended by, if necessary, striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 9(3) and (4), sections 36.1, 38.1 and 45.1, and subsection 56(5), as enacted by the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1.
If subsection 343(2) of the other Act comes into force before the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 233 of this Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 38(2)(e) and (f) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that, at the filing date of the application in Canada, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), the applicant was not using and did not propose to use the trademark in Canada in association with the goods or services specified in the application; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that, at the filing date of the application in Canada, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), the applicant was not entitled to use the trademark in Canada in association with those goods or services.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 343(2) of the other Act comes into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then section 233 of this Act is deemed to come into force before that subsection 343(2).
If section 357 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 227 of this Act, then that section 357 is deemed to have come into force before that section 227.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: first Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1. (première loi)
second Act means the Combating Counterfeit Products Act. (deuxième loi)

Subsection.Text: If subsection 326(4) of the first Act or subsection 15(4) of the second Act comes into force on or before the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 231 and 236 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 12(3) of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A trademark that is not registrable by reason of paragraph (1)(a) or (b) is registrable if it is distinctive at the filing date of an application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), having regard to all the circumstances of the case including the length of time during which it has been used.

If subsection 367(48) of the first Act produces its effects on or before the day on which this Act receives royal assent, or if section 339 of the first Act and section 31 of the second Act are both in force on that day, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 232 and 237 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 32(1) of the Trademarks Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant shall furnish the Registrar with any evidence that the Registrar may require establishing that the trademark is distinctive at the filing date of the application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), if any of the following apply:
If section 31 of the second Act comes into force on or before the day on which this Act receives royal assent and section 339 of the first Act is not in force on that day, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 237 of this Act is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 32(1) of the Trade-marks Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An applicant shall furnish the Registrar with any evidence that the Registrar may require establishing that the trade-mark is distinctive at the filing date of the application for its registration, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1), if any of the following apply:

If section 344 of the first Act and section 36 of the second Act are both in force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, or if subsection 367(59) or (61) of the first Act has produced its effects on or before that day, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 234, 235 and 238 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 39(1)(a) and (b) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Subsection.Text: If section 36 of the second Act comes into force on or before the day on which this Act receives royal assent and section 344 of the first Act is not in force on that day, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sections 234, 235 and 238 of this Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 39.1(1)(a) and (b) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 344 of the first Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 39(1)(a) and (b) that it enacts by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on its filing date, determined without taking into account subsection 34(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within the scope of the original application on the day on which the divisional application is filed, if the divisional application is filed on or after the day on which the original application is advertised under subsection 37(1).

Subsection.Text: Section 214 comes into force on the first day on which both section 340 of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 and section 33 of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act are in force or, if it is later, on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Sections 215 to 217, 219, 221 to 223 and 225 to 228 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must not be before the day fixed under subsection 368(1) of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1.

Sections 220, 224, 229 and 230 come into force on the day fixed under subsection 368(1) of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 or, if it is later, on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Section.Text: Section 41.25 of the Copyright Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: A notice of claimed infringement shall not contain

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offer to settle the claimed infringement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a request or demand, made in relation to the claimed infringement, for payment or for personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a reference, including by way of hyperlink, to such an offer, request or demand; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that may be prescribed by regulation.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 41.26(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person described in paragraph 41.25(1)(a) or (b) who receives a notice of claimed infringement that complies with subsections 41.25(2) and (3) shall, on being paid any fee that the person has lawfully charged for doing so,

Section.Text: Subsection 41.27(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the provider receives a notice of claimed infringement, relating to a work or other subject-matter, that complies with subsections 41.25(2) and (3) after the work or other subject-matter has been removed from the electronic location set out in the notice, then subsection (1) applies, with respect to reproductions made from that electronic location, only to infringements that occurred before the day that is 30 days — or the period that may be prescribed by regulation — after the day on which the provider receives the notice.

Section.Text: Paragraph 62(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the form of a notice of claimed infringement referred to in section 41.25 and prescribing the information that must be and that is not permitted to be contained in it;

TitleText.XRefExternal: College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act

Section.Text: The College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Board means the board of directors established under section 13. (conseil)
College means the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents established by section 5. (Collège)
investigator means an individual designated under section 42. (enquêteur)
licence means a licence issued under this Act. (permis)
licensee means a patent agent or a trade-mark agent. (titulaire de permis)
Minister means the federal minister designated under section 4. (ministre)
patent agent means an individual who holds a patent agent licence or a patent agent in training licence issued under section 26. (agent de brevets)

privileged means protected by litigation privilege, by solicitor-client privilege, by the professional secrecy of advocates and notaries or by the privilege set out in section 16.1 of the Patent Act or section 51.13 of the Trade-marks Act. (protégé)
Registrar means the Registrar of the College appointed under section 22. (registraire)
trade-mark agent means an individual who holds a trade-mark agent licence or a trade-mark agent in training licence issued under section 29. (agent de marques de commerce)

Section.Text: This Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province.
The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any federal minister to be the Minister for the purposes of this Act.

Subsection.Text: A corporation without share capital is established, to be known as the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents.
The Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act does not apply to the College.

Section.Text: The purpose of the College is to regulate patent agents and trade-mark agents in the public interest, in order to enhance the public’s ability to secure the rights provided for under the Patent Act and the Trade-marks Act.
The head office of the College is to be in the National Capital Region, as described in the schedule to the National Capital Act.
In carrying out its purpose, the College has the capacity and the rights, powers and privileges of a natural person.

The College is not an agent of Her Majesty, and the College’s directors, the members of its committees, the Registrar, the investigators and any officers, employees, agents and mandataries of the College are not part of the federal public administration.
The Official Languages Act applies to the College.
The members of the College are the licensees.
The College must hold an annual general meeting of members within six months after the end of each calendar year, at a time and place in Canada fixed by the Board.

Subsection.Text: The College has a board of directors composed of at least seven directors, including the Chairperson.
The Minister must, by order, fix the number of directors.
The Minister may, by order, fix the number of directors that are to be appointed to the Board and appoint those directors.
The number of appointed directors fixed under subsection (3) must not be greater than the minimum number that is required to constitute a majority of directors on the Board.
The remaining directors are to be licensees elected in accordance with the by-laws, and at least one elected director must be a patent agent and at least one must be a trade-mark agent.

Section.Text: An individual is not eligible to be appointed or elected as a director if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are less than 18 years of age;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have the status of bankrupt;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: are a member of an association whose primary purpose is to represent the interests of persons who provide advice on patents or trade-marks;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: were, within the preceding 12 months, a member of a governing body or a steering committee of an association referred to in paragraph (c);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appointment to the Board,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are a licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are employed by a department, as defined in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of election to the Board,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: have a licence that is suspended, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: meet any other ineligibility criteria set out in the by-laws.

Subsection.Text: Each director is to be appointed or elected for a term of not more than three years, and may be reappointed or re-elected for subsequent terms of not more than three years each.
The length of a director’s term is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: set out by the Minister in the order appointing the director, if the director is appointed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: determined in accordance with the by-laws, if the director is elected.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, it is not necessary that all directors hold office for terms that begin or end on the same day.
Despite subsection (1) and subject to section 17, an appointed director continues to hold office until they are re-appointed or their successor is appointed.
An appointed director holds office during good behaviour and may be removed for cause by the Minister.
An elected director may be removed in accordance with the by-laws.

Section.Text: A director ceases to be a director if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: die;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: resign;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: become bankrupt;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: become a member of an association whose primary purpose is to represent the interests of persons who provide advice on patents or trade-marks;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: become a member of a governing body or a steering committee of an association referred to in paragraph (d);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: are removed from office under section 16;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an appointed director,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: become a licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: become an employee of a department, as defined in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an elected director,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: have a licence that is suspended,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: cease to be a licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: meet the ineligibility criteria set out in the by-laws.

Section.Text: The College may pay to the directors the remuneration and expenses that are determined in accordance with the by-laws.

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson is to be elected by the Board from among the directors in accordance with the by-laws.
The Chairperson presides over meetings of the Board and performs any other duties that are assigned to the Chairperson by the by-laws.
The Board may remove the Chairperson in accordance with the by-laws.
The Board must hold a meeting at least once every calendar year.
Subject to the by-laws, meetings of the Board are open to the public.

Heading.TitleText: Committees, Registrar and Chief Executive Officer

Subsection.Text: Two committees of the College are established, to be known as the Investigations Committee and the Discipline Committee.
The members of each committee are to be appointed by the Board in accordance with the regulations.
An individual is not permitted to be a member of either committee if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are a member of an association whose primary purpose is to represent the interests of persons who provide advice on patents or trade-marks; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: they are a member of a governing body or a committee of an association referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: An individual is not permitted to be a member of the Discipline Committee and the Investigations Committee at the same time.

Section.Text: The Board must appoint a Registrar of the College, who is responsible for the Register of Patent Agents and the Register of Trade-mark Agents.
The Board may appoint a Chief Executive Officer, who is responsible for the College’s day-to-day operations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: review the Board’s activities and require the Board to provide reports and information, other than privileged information, to the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: require the Board to do anything — including to make, amend or repeal a regulation or by-law — that, in the Minister’s opinion, is advisable to carry out the purposes of this Act.

Subsection.Text: Compliance by a director of the Board with a requirement of the Minister is deemed to be in the best interests of the College.
On or before March 31 of each year, the College must submit to the Minister a report on the College’s activities during the preceding calendar year.
The Minister must cause a copy of the report to be tabled in each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the Minister receives it.
On application, the Registrar must issue a patent agent licence to an individual who meets the requirements under the regulations.
On application, the Registrar must issue a patent agent in training licence to an individual who meets the requirements under the regulations.
A licence issued under this section is subject to any conditions imposed under this Act.

Section.Text: Subject to any restrictions imposed under this Act, a patent agent whose licence is not suspended is entitled to represent persons in the presentation and prosecution of applications for patents or in other business before the Patent Office.

Subsection.Text: The Register of Patent Agents must include the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and contact information of every patent agent and the name and contact information of any firm of which the patent agent is a member;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for every patent agent, any conditions that are imposed on their licence under this Act and any restrictions that are imposed under this Act on their entitlement to provide representation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a patent agent whose licence is suspended, an indication that their licence is suspended and the date on which it was suspended;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for every patent agent, all disciplinary measures imposed on them by the Discipline Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the name of every individual whose patent agent licence or patent agent in training licence was surrendered or revoked; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other information required by the regulations or by-laws.

Subsection.Text: The Registrar must ensure that the information included in the Register is updated in a timely manner.
The Register must be made available to the public on the College’s website in a searchable format.

The Registrar must provide the Commissioner of Patents, within the time and in the form and manner that are specified by the Commissioner, with the most recent information referred to in paragraph (1)(a), other than information relating to a patent agent whose licence is suspended.
On application, the Registrar must issue a trade-mark agent licence to an individual who meets the requirements under the regulations.
On application, the Registrar must issue a trade-mark agent in training licence to an individual who meets the requirements under the regulations.
A licence issued under this section is subject to any conditions imposed under this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Representation before Office of the Registrar of Trade-marks
Section.Text: Subject to any restrictions imposed under this Act, a trade-mark agent whose licence is not suspended is entitled to represent persons in the presentation and prosecution of applications for the registration of trade-marks or in other business before the Office of the Registrar of Trade-marks.

Subsection.Text: The Register of Trade-mark Agents must include the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and contact information of every trade-mark agent and the name and contact information of any firm of which the trade-mark agent is a member;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for every trade-mark agent, any conditions that are imposed on their licence under this Act and any restrictions that are imposed under this Act on their entitlement to provide representation;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a trade-mark agent whose licence is suspended, an indication that their licence is suspended and the date on which it was suspended;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for every trade-mark agent, all disciplinary measures imposed on them by the Discipline Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the name of every individual whose trade-mark agent licence or trade-mark agent in training licence was surrendered or revoked; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other information required by the regulations or by-laws.

Subsection.Text: The Registrar must ensure that the information included in the Register is updated in a timely manner.
The Register must be made available to the public on the College’s website in a searchable format.

The Registrar must provide the Registrar of Trade-marks, within the time and in the form and manner that are specified by the Registrar of Trade-marks, with the most recent information referred to in paragraph (1)(a), other than information relating to a trade-mark agent whose licence is suspended.

Section.MarginalNote: Standards of professional conduct and competence
Section.Text: A licensee must meet the standards of professional conduct and competence that are established by the code of professional conduct. A licensee who fails to meet those standards commits professional misconduct or is incompetent.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must, by regulation, establish a code of professional conduct for licensees.
Only the Board may, by regulation and with the Minister’s prior written approval, amend or repeal the regulations establishing the code.
Subject to subsections (2) and (3), a licensee must be insured against professional liability.

A licensee who is employed by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province, or by an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada or an agent or mandatary of Her Majesty in right of a province, is not required to be insured against professional liability for work done as a licensee within the scope of that employment.
A licensee may be exempted by the by-laws from the application of subsection (1).

Heading.TitleText: Suspension, Revocation and Surrender of Licences

Subsection.Text: The Registrar may, in accordance with the by-laws, suspend a licence if the licensee fails to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pay the annual fee in accordance with the by-laws;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: pay, within the time and in the manner provided for under this Act, any other fee or amount that the licensee is required to pay under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide any information or document in accordance with the by-laws;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any requirement that is imposed by the by-laws with respect to continuing professional development;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: comply with a condition of their licence that is imposed under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any requirement with respect to professional liability insurance that is imposed under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any other requirement that is imposed under the by-laws.

Subsection.Text: The Registrar must notify a licensee in writing if their licence is suspended under this section.

A licence is suspended under this section until the licensee complies with the requirements set out in the by-laws to address the failure that led to the suspension or until the licence is revoked by the Registrar under subsection (4).
The Registrar may, in accordance with the by-laws, revoke a suspended licence if it has been suspended under this section for the applicable period of time that is specified in the by-laws.

Section.Text: The Registrar may, in accordance with by-laws, approve the surrender of a licence on application by a licensee made in accordance with the by-laws.

The Investigations Committee must, on the basis of a complaint or on its own initiative, conduct an investigation into a licensee’s conduct and activities if it has reasonable grounds to believe that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent.

The Investigations Committee must give notice, before the conclusion of the investigation and in accordance with the by-laws, of the nature of an investigation to the licensee who is its subject and give the licensee a period of at least 30 days after the date of the notice within which they may make written representations to the Committee.
The Investigations Committee must consider all complaints received by the College that, in the opinion of the Committee, relate to professional misconduct or incompetence by a licensee.

Subsection.Text: If, after considering a complaint, the Investigations Committee does not have reasonable grounds to believe that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent, the Committee must dismiss the complaint and notify the complainant in writing of the dismissal and the reasons for it.
The Investigations Committee is not permitted to disclose privileged information in its notice to the complainant.

Section.Text: With the complainant’s consent, the Investigations Committee may refer a complaint that it has dismissed to a body that has a statutory duty to regulate a profession.

Subsection.Text: The Investigations Committee may designate an individual as an investigator to conduct an investigation under the Committee’s direction.
The Investigations Committee may revoke the designation.
For the purpose of investigating a licensee’s conduct and activities, an investigator may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (3), enter the licensee’s business premises at any reasonable time and require the production of any document or other thing that is relevant to the investigation, and examine or copy the document or thing or remove it for examination or copying; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: require the licensee, or a business partner or employee of the licensee or any person employed by the same employer as the licensee, to provide any information that is relevant to the investigation.

Subsection.Text: An investigator may take any action under subsection (1) with respect to any privileged information, including privileged information contained in a document or other thing, unless the information

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is subject to litigation privilege or solicitor-client privilege or the professional secrecy of advocates and notaries;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not protected by the privilege set out in section 16.1 of the Patent Act or section 51.13 of the Trade-marks Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not related to a patent, a trade-mark, a geographical indication or a mark referred to in paragraph 9(1)(e), (i), (i.1), (i.3), (n) or (n.1) of the Trade-marks Act.

Subsection.Text: If the licensee’s business premises are in a dwelling-house, the investigator may enter them only with the occupant’s consent or under the authority of a warrant issued under section 44.

An investigator may remove a document or other thing from business premises under paragraph (1)(a) only if the investigator gives a person who appears to be in control of the premises a receipt for the document or thing.
On the application of an investigator, the Federal Court may issue a warrant authorizing an investigator to do anything set out in subsection (2) if the Court is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that a licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that there are documents or things in a place, including a dwelling-house, that are relevant to an investigation into the licensee’s conduct or activities; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the warrant is necessary because

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the matter is urgent and that it would be impractical to carry out the investigation using only other investigative procedures,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the powers conferred by section 43 have been or are likely to be ineffective,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that information in the documents or other things meets all the conditions set out in paragraphs 43(2)(a) to (c), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 43 does not authorize the investigator to enter the place.

Subsection.Text: A warrant may authorize an investigator, subject to any conditions set out in the warrant, to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enter and search the place referred to in paragraph (1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: examine or copy any document or other thing that is relevant to the investigation and that is in the place, including any document or thing that contains information that is privileged; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: remove from the place any document or other thing that is relevant to the investigation, including any document or thing that contains information that is privileged.

Subsection.Text: An investigator who removes a document or other thing from a place under the warrant must give a receipt for the document or thing to a person who appears to be in control of the place.
In executing a warrant, an investigator may use force only if they are named in the warrant, are expressly authorized by it to use force and are accompanied by a peace officer.
An application under subsection (1) may be made ex parte.

On the application of an investigator, the Federal Court may make an order requiring a person to produce any document or other thing for examination, copying or removal by an investigator or to provide any information to an investigator, subject to any conditions that are specified in the order, if the Court is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that a licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the document, thing or information is relevant to an investigation into the licensee’s conduct or activities; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the order is necessary because

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the matter is urgent and that it would be impractical to carry out the investigation using only other investigative procedures,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the powers conferred by section 43 have been or are likely to be ineffective,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 43(1)(b) does not authorize the investigator to require the person to provide the information, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the document or thing contains information that meets, or the information meets, all the conditions set out in paragraphs 43(2)(a) to (c).
