Subsection.Text: An order under subsection (1) may be made with respect to any privileged information, including privileged information contained in a document or other thing.
An application under subsection (1) may be made ex parte.
An investigator who removes a document or other thing produced under an order must give a receipt for the document or thing to the person who produces it.

If an investigator requires the production of a document or other thing — or is about to examine, copy or remove it — under section 43, under a warrant issued under section 44 or under an order made under section 45, and the document or thing is in the possession of legal counsel or a firm of legal counsel,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the investigator must not examine or copy the document or thing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: legal counsel or a representative of the firm must place the document or thing in a package and seal and identify the package in accordance with the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the investigator must retain and remove the package once it is sealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: legal counsel or the firm must take all reasonable steps

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to notify the holder of a privilege with respect to the document or thing within the period prescribed by regulation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the holder cannot be located within that period, to immediately notify the law society of the applicable province or the Chambre des notaires du Québec, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If an investigator requires the production of a document or other thing — or is about to examine, copy or remove it — under section 43, under a warrant issued under section 44 or under an order made under section 45, a person in possession of the document or thing may object to its production, examination, copying or removal on one or more of the following grounds:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the document or thing is not relevant to the investigation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the investigator is acting under section 43, the document or thing contains information that meets all the conditions set out in paragraphs 43(2)(a) to (c);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the investigator is acting under the authority of a warrant or order, the production, examination, copying or removal of the document or thing is not authorized by the warrant or order.

Subsection.Text: If the person makes an objection under subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the investigator must not examine or copy the document or thing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person must place the document or thing in a package and seal and identify the package in accordance with the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the investigator must retain and remove the package once it is sealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the person is not the owner of the document or thing or the holder of a privilege with respect to it, the person must immediately notify the owner or holder of the objection.

Subsection.Text: The legal counsel or firm referred to in subsection (1), the person who makes the objection under subsection (2) or any other person who has a right or interest in the document or other thing that is sealed may apply to the Federal Court in accordance with the regulations for a determination of whether any of the grounds set out in paragraphs (2)(a) to (c) apply with respect to the document or thing.
The sealed package is to be retained, opened or returned only in accordance with the regulations.

Section.MarginalNote: Return or application for retention order

Subsection.Text: An investigator who, as a result of the application of any of sections 43 to 45 or the regulations referred to in subsection 46(5), is in possession of a document or other thing that is not in a sealed package must, within the applicable period determined in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 76(1)(i), return the document or thing or apply to the Federal Court for a retention order under subsection (2).

On application by the investigator, the Federal Court may order that the document or thing may be retained by the investigator until no later than the conclusion of the investigation or, if an application is made to the Discipline Committee under subsection 49(1), until the final disposition of the proceedings before the Discipline Committee and any appeals. If the Court does not order that the document or thing be retained, the document or thing must be returned as soon as feasible.

A person who has a right or interest in a document or other thing that, as a result of the application of any of sections 43 to 45 or the regulations referred to in subsection 46(5), is in the possession of an investigator and that is not in a sealed package may, on giving notice to the Investigations Committee, apply to the Federal Court for an order that the document or thing be returned or that any copies of the document or thing made by or on behalf of the Committee or an investigator be destroyed.
The Court may order, on any conditions that it considers appropriate,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the document or thing be returned; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that any copies of the document or thing made by or on behalf of the Investigations Committee or an investigator be destroyed, if the removal of the document or thing by an investigator was not authorized under this Act.

Subsection.Text: If, at the conclusion of an investigation, the Investigations Committee is satisfied that there is sufficient evidence that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent, the Committee must apply to the Discipline Committee for a decision as to whether the licensee committed professional misconduct or was incompetent. Otherwise, the Investigations Committee must dismiss the matter.
The Investigations Committee must consider any written representations made by the licensee under section 38 before the conclusion of the investigation.
The Investigations Committee must, in writing, notify the licensee and any complainant of the application or dismissal and, in the case of dismissal, the reasons for it.
The Investigations Committee is not permitted to disclose privileged information in its notice to the licensee or complainant.

Section.Text: The Investigations Committee may withdraw an application to the Discipline Committee only if the Investigations Committee is no longer satisfied that there is sufficient evidence that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent.

The Discipline Committee must hold an oral hearing for every application made by the Investigations Committee in order to determine whether a licensee committed professional misconduct or was incompetent.
Except to the extent necessary to protect privileged or confidential information, the hearings of the Discipline Committee are open to the public.
The Investigations Committee and the licensee are parties to the application.

Section.MarginalNote: Right of complainant to make submissions
Section.Text: If the application results from a complaint, the complainant is entitled to make oral and written submissions to the Discipline Committee.

Subsection.Text: The Discipline Committee has the power

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to summon and enforce the appearance of persons and compel them to give oral or written evidence on oath and to produce any document or other thing that the Committee considers necessary to decide the application, in the same manner and to the same extent as a superior court of record;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to administer oaths; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to accept any evidence, whether admissible in a court of law or not.

Subsection.Text: The Discipline Committee may take any action referred to in subsection (1) with respect to information that is privileged.

Before making a decision under section 57, the Discipline Committee may take any action referred to in any of paragraphs 57(3)(a) to (c) if the Discipline Committee is satisfied that it is necessary for the protection of the public.
Any action taken under subsection (1) is provisional and ceases to have effect

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: after the decision on the application is made, unless the action is confirmed in the decision; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the application is withdrawn.

Subsection.Text: After the hearing of an application, the Discipline Committee must decide whether the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent.
If the Discipline Committee decides that the licensee did not commit professional misconduct or was not incompetent, the Committee must dismiss the application.
If the Discipline Committee decides that the licensee has committed professional misconduct or was incompetent, the Committee may, in its decision,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: impose conditions on a licence of the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: impose restrictions on the licensee’s entitlement to represent persons under section 27 or 30;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: suspend a licence of the licensee for a period of not more than two years or until specified conditions are met, or both;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: revoke a licence of the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: reprimand the licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: require the licensee to pay a penalty of not more than $10,000 to the College;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: require the licensee to pay to the College or any complainant all or a portion of the costs incurred by them during the application before the Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: require the licensee to reimburse all or a portion of the fees or disbursements paid to the licensee by a client; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: take or require any other action that the Committee considers appropriate in the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: The Discipline Committee must give its decision and the reasons for it in writing.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Decision and reasons available to public
Subsection.Text: A decision and the reasons for it, other than any privileged and confidential information contained in the decision or reasons, must be made available to the public on the College’s website.
The Discipline Committee must provide a copy of every decision made under section 57 to the Registrar.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to licensees — revocation or suspension
Subsection.Text: The Registrar must notify all licensees of every decision made under subsection 57(3) to revoke or suspend a licence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to licensees — dismissal of application
Subsection.Text: The Registrar must, if requested by the licensee who was the subject of an application to the Discipline Committee that was dismissed, notify all licensees of the dismissal.

Section.Text: A party to an application may appeal a decision of the Discipline Committee made under section 57 to the Federal Court within 30 days after the day on which the decision is made.

Section.MarginalNote: Filing of decision in Federal Court

Subsection.Text: The College may file in the Federal Court a certified copy of a final decision of the Discipline Committee made under section 57 that is not subject to appeal.
On the certified copy being filed, the decision becomes and may be enforced as an order of the Federal Court.

If the Discipline Committee suspends a licence under paragraph 57(3)(c) until specified conditions are met, the Committee must, on application by the licensee, lift the suspension if the Committee is satisfied that the conditions are met.
An application under subsection (1) may be made ex parte.
The Discipline Committee must provide written notice to the Registrar as soon as feasible after it lifts the suspension.

The Registrar may, at any time after the second anniversary of the day on which a licence is suspended under paragraph 57(3)(c) until specified conditions are met and after giving at least 30 days’ written notice to the licensee, revoke the licence if the suspension has not been lifted under subsection (1).
The Registrar must notify the licensee in writing as soon as feasible after their licence is revoked.

Section.Text: Amounts paid to the College as a penalty under paragraph 57(3)(f) may be expended only for the purpose of supporting the mental health of licensees.
The Discipline Committee may make rules respecting the practice and procedure before it and rules for carrying out its work and for the management of its internal affairs.

For greater certainty, the disclosure of privileged information to the College, including to the Investigations Committee or to the Discipline Committee, or to an investigator does not constitute a waiver of the privilege.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the following persons must not disclose a complaint received by the College or any privileged or confidential information obtained in the course of an investigation or proceeding under this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former director of the Board;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former member of the Investigations Committee or of the Discipline Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Registrar or a former Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former investigator;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a current or former officer, employee, agent or mandatary of the College;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is or has been engaged by the College.

Subsection.Text: A person referred to in subsection (1) may disclose a complaint or information referred to in that subsection if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is for the purpose of permitting the exercise of powers or the performance of duties or functions under this Act, including in connection with a proceeding under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information is available to the public;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is to the person’s legal counsel;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the person has obtained the written consent of all persons whose rights or interests might reasonably be affected by the disclosure; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: there is a significant risk of harm to any person if the disclosure is not made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: making the disclosure is likely to reduce the risk.

Subsection.Text: A person referred to in subsection (1) is not required, in any proceeding other than a proceeding under this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to give testimony on a complaint received by the College or on any privileged or confidential information that the person is prohibited from disclosing under that subsection; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to produce such a complaint or any document or other thing that contains any such privileged or confidential information.

Subsection.Text: The College may apply to the Federal Court for an order authorizing the disclosure of confidential information that would be prohibited by section 65 to a law enforcement agency or other public authority.

The Court must not make an order if the information is privileged or if it was obtained by the College as a result of a person making, in the course of an investigation or proceeding under this Act, an oral or written statement that may tend to incriminate the person.
An order that authorizes the disclosure of information may also authorize the delivery of documents or other things that are in the possession of the College and that relate to the information.

Section.Text: A person, other than a patent agent whose licence is not suspended, must not

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use the title “patent agent” or a variation or abbreviation of that title, or any words, name or designation, in a manner that leads to a reasonable belief that the person is a patent agent; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: represent themselves, in any way or by any means, to be a patent agent.

Section.Text: A person, other than a trade-mark agent whose licence is not suspended, must not

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use the title “trade-mark agent” or a variation or abbreviation of that title, or any words, name or designation, in a manner that leads to a reasonable belief that the person is a trade-mark agent; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: represent themselves, in any way or by any means, to be a trade-mark agent.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes section 67 or 68 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $25,000 for a first offence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $50,000 for a second or subsequent offence.

Subsection.Text: If a person is convicted of an offence under subsection (1), no imprisonment is to be imposed as punishment for the offence or in default of payment of any fine imposed as punishment in relation to the offence.
A person is not to be found guilty of an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

Subject to subsection (2) and the regulations, a person must not represent another person in the presentation and prosecution of applications for patents or in other business before the Patent Office.

Subsection (1) does not apply to a patent agent whose licence is not suspended, to legal counsel who is providing legal services as authorized by law or to a person who is part of a class of persons exempted under the regulations.

Section.MarginalNote: Unauthorized representation before Office of the Registrar of Trade-marks

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and the regulations, a person must not represent another person in the presentation and prosecution of applications for the registration of trade-marks or in other business before the Office of the Registrar of Trade-marks.

Subsection (1) does not apply to a trade-mark agent whose licence is not suspended, to legal counsel who is providing legal services as authorized by law or to a person who is part of a class of persons exempted under the regulations.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting an activity, a class of persons or an activity that is performed by a class of persons from the application of subsection 70(1) or 71(1).

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes section 70 or 71 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for a first offence, to a fine of not more than $25,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for a second or subsequent offence, to a fine of not more than $50,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both.

Subsection.Text: A person is not to be found guilty of an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

Section.Text: On application by the College, if the Federal Court is satisfied that a contravention of section 67, 68, 70 or 71 is being or is likely to be committed, the Court may grant an injunction, subject to any conditions that it considers appropriate, ordering any person to cease or refrain from any activity related to that contravention or ordering the person to take any measure that the Court considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: The Board may make by-laws respecting any matter necessary to carry on the activities of the College, including by-laws

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the election of directors, the terms of elected directors and their removal;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing ineligibility criteria for the purposes of subparagraphs 14(f)(ii) and 17(h)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the filling of vacancies caused by the temporary absence or incapacity of elected directors;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the remuneration and expenses of directors;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the election and removal of the Chairperson of the Board and the Chairperson’s duties;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duties and functions of the Board and the meetings of the Board, including quorum;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conflicts of interest of directors, members of the Investigations Committee and members of the Discipline Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duties and functions of the Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the maintenance of the Register of Patent Agents and the Register of Trade-mark Agents and information that is required to be included in those Registers;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the classes of firm in connection with which a licensee may work as a licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: fixing the annual fee — or the manner of determining the annual fee — that is to be paid by licensees;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: fixing any other fee — or the manner of determining any other fee — that is to be paid by licensees, including any fee for the late payment of another fee;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the time within which and the manner in which any fees or other amounts are to be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information and documents that licensees must provide to the College;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: respecting continuing professional development requirements for licensees;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: respecting requirements for licensees to do pro bono work;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the professional liability insurance that licensees are required to maintain;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: exempting licensees from the requirement to be insured against professional liability;

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the suspension and revocation of licences under section 35;

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the surrender of licences and applications for a surrender; and

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the notification of licensees under section 38.

Subsection.Text: The by-laws made under paragraphs (1)(j) to (u) may distinguish among classes of licensees or licences.
For greater certainty, by-laws made under paragraphs (1)(j) to (u) are regulations for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the Investigations Committee, the Discipline Committee and other committees of the College, including the composition of those committees, the eligibility for membership in them, the terms of the members and their removal;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reports and information that must be provided or submitted to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting conditions to be imposed on licences or classes of licences;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the requirements that individuals or classes of individuals must meet under section 26 or 29, including requirements in relation to qualifying examinations and in relation to fees with respect to qualifying examinations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting what constitutes representation for the purposes of sections 27 and 70 or for the purposes of sections 30 and 71;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting restrictions on the entitlement of licensees or classes of licensees to provide representation under section 27 or 30;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information to be included in the Register of Patent Agents or the Register of Trade-mark Agents;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sealing of documents and other things and objections under section 46, including in relation to notifications, the identification of sealed packages, applications to the Federal Court and the retention, opening and return of sealed packages; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the determination of the applicable periods referred to in subsection 47(1).

Subsection.Text: Regulations made under paragraphs (1)(c), (d), (f) and (g) may authorize the College to make by-laws with respect to all or part of the subject matter of the regulations and, for greater certainty, those by-laws are regulations for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.
Regulations made under paragraph (1)(h) may authorize the Federal Court to make orders respecting the retention, opening or return of sealed packages.

Section.Text: The regulations prevail over the by-laws to the extent of an inconsistency or conflict between them.
In sections 79 to 86, coming-into-force day means the day on which section 13 comes into force.

Subsection.Text: Before the coming-into-force day, the College’s board of directors is to be composed of five directors appointed by the Minister of Industry.

Two of the directors are to be appointed on the recommendation of the Intellectual Property Institute of Canada, with one to be an individual whose name is on the register kept under section 15 of the Patent Act and the other to be an individual whose name is on the list of trade-mark agents kept under section 28 of the Trade-marks Act.
The Minister of Industry must designate a Chairperson from among the directors.

The directors appointed on the recommendation of the Intellectual Property Institute of Canada who hold office on the coming-into-force day are deemed to have been elected under subsection 13(5) on that day, for a term that ends on the earlier of the day on which the first election is held under that subsection and the first anniversary of the coming-into-force day. The other directors who hold office on the coming-into-force day are deemed to have been appointed under subsection 13(3) on that day for a term that ends on the first anniversary of that day.
For greater certainty, section 17 applies as of the coming-into-force day with respect to directors who are deemed to be elected or appointed under subsection (4).
Before the coming-into-force day, a director appointed under subsection (1) may be removed for cause by the Minister of Industry.
Before the coming-into-force day, the College may pay the remuneration and expenses that it fixes to the directors.
Any reference in this Act to the Board is, until the coming-into-force day, a reference to the board of directors as constituted under this section.

Section.Text: Any by-laws that are made by the Board before the first election is held under subsection 13(5) are repealed on the 180th day after the day on which the first election is held, unless they are confirmed by a resolution of the Board, as it is constituted after that election, before that 180th day.
Despite Part X of the Financial Administration Act, the College is not a Crown corporation within the meaning of that Act.

An individual is deemed to have been issued a patent agent licence under subsection 26(1) on the coming-into-force day if their name was, on the day before the coming-into-force day, on the register kept under section 15 of the Patent Act.

An individual is deemed to have been issued a trade-mark agent licence under subsection 29(1) on the coming-into-force day if their name was, on the day before the coming-into-force day, on the list of trade-mark agents kept under section 28 of the Trade-marks Act.

The Commissioner of Patents may, for the purpose of enabling the College or the Registrar to exercise their powers or perform their duties and functions under this Act, provide the Registrar with information relating to an individual or firm whose name is or was on the register kept under section 15 of the Patent Act, an individual who has sat for the qualifying examination for patent agents or an individual who has notified the Commissioner in writing of their intention to sit for the examination, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual’s or firm’s name and contact information and, in the case of an individual, the name of any firm of which they are a member;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the Commissioner has refused to recognize the individual or firm as a patent agent or attorney under section 16 of the Patent Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any complaints against the individual or firm; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the results of any qualifying examinations for patent agents.

Section.Text: The Registrar of Trade-marks may, for the purpose of enabling the College or the Registrar to exercise their powers or perform their duties and functions under this Act, provide the Registrar with information relating to an individual or firm whose name is or was on the list of trade-mark agents kept under section 28 of the Trade-marks Act, an individual who has sat for the qualifying examination for trade-mark agents or an individual who has notified the Registrar of Trade-marks in writing of their intention to sit for the examination, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual’s or firm’s name and contact information and, in the case of an individual, the name of any firm of which they are a member;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any complaints against the individual or firm; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the results of any qualifying examinations for trade-mark agents.

Section.Text: Without limiting the generality of section 76, the Governor in Council may make regulations respecting transitional matters relating to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: qualifying examinations for patent agents and trade-mark agents;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the requirements to be met under section 26 by an individual who has sat for the qualifying examination for patent agents before the coming-into-force day or who has, before that day, notified the Commissioner of Patents in writing of their intention to sit for the examination but whose name was not, on the day before that day, on the register kept under section 15 of the Patent Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the requirements to be met under section 29 by an individual who has sat for the qualifying examination for trade-mark agents before the coming-into-force day or who has, before that day, notified the Registrar of Trade-marks in writing of their intention to sit for the examination but whose name was not, on the day before that day, on the list of trade-mark agents kept under section 28 of the Trade-marks Act.

Section.Text: Schedule I to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Collège des agents de brevets et des agents de marques de commerce

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Patent Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: patent agent has the same meaning as in section 2 of the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act; (agent de brevets)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 12(1)(j) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Paragraph.Label: (j.001)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the submission, including in electronic form and by electronic means, of documents and information to the Commissioner or the Patent Office, including the time at which they are deemed to be received by the Commissioner or the Patent Office;

Paragraph.Label: (j.002)
Paragraph.Text: respecting communications between the Commissioner and any other person;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j.002):

Paragraph.Label: (j.003)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an applicant, patentee or other person may or must be represented in business before the Patent Office by a patent agent whose licence is not suspended or by another person;

Section.Text: Sections 15 and 16 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 16.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is between a patent agent and their client;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 16.1(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Patent agents — country other than Canada
Subsection.Text: A communication between an individual who is authorized to act as the equivalent of a patent agent under the law of a country other than Canada and that individual’s client that is privileged under the law of that other country and that would be privileged under subsection (1) had it been made between a patent agent and their client is deemed to be a communication that meets the conditions set out in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Individual acting on behalf of patent agent or client
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, a patent agent or an individual who is authorized to act as the equivalent of a patent agent under the law of a country other than Canada includes an individual acting on their behalf and a client includes an individual acting on the client’s behalf.

Section.Text: Section 78.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Applications for patents in Canada filed before October 1, 1989 shall be dealt with and disposed of in accordance with section 38.1 and with the provisions of this Act, other than section 15, as they read immediately before October 1, 1989.
The schedule to the Privacy Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Other Government Institutions”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Collège des agents de brevets et des agents de marques de commerce

Section.Text: Section 28 of the Trade-marks Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 29(1)(c) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 29(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Registrar shall, on request and on payment of the prescribed fee, furnish a copy certified by the Registrar of any entry in the register or list, or of any of those applications, requests or documents.
Paragraph 51.13(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is between a trade-mark agent and their client;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 51.13(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Trade-mark agents — country other than Canada
Subsection.Text: A communication between an individual who is authorized to act as the equivalent of a trade-mark agent under the law of a country other than Canada and that individual’s client that is privileged under the law of that other country and that would be privileged under subsection (1) had it been made between a trade-mark agent and their client is deemed to be a communication that meets the conditions set out in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Individual acting on behalf of trade-mark agent or client
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, a trade-mark agent or an individual who is authorized to act as the equivalent of a trade-mark agent under the law of a country other than Canada includes an individual acting on their behalf and a client includes an individual acting on the client’s behalf.
Section 51.13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):
In this section, trade-mark agent has the same meaning as in section 2 of the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act.
Paragraph 65(c.1) of the Act is repealed.
Section 65 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d.1) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of documents, fees and information to the Registrar, including the time at which they are deemed to be received by the Registrar; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: communications between the Registrar and any other person.

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2.
If subsection 118(4) of the other Act comes into force before subsection 250(2) of this Act, then that subsection 250(2) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

If subsection 250(2) of this Act comes into force before subsection 118(4) of the other Act, then, on the day on which that subsection 118(4) comes into force, paragraphs 12(1)(j.001) and (j.002) of the Patent Act are repealed.
If subsection 118(4) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 250(2) of this Act, then that subsection 250(2) is deemed never to come into force and is repealed.
If subsection 118(4) of the other Act comes into force before subsection 250(3) of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 250(3) is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which section 249 of this Act comes into force, paragraph 12(1)(j.01) of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an applicant, patentee or other person may or must be represented in business before the Patent Office by a patent agent whose licence is not suspended or by another person;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 250(3) of this Act comes into force before subsection 118(4) of the other Act, then, on the day on which that subsection 118(4) comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 12(1)(j.003) of the Patent Act is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 12(1)(j.01) of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an applicant, patentee or other person may or must be represented in business before the Patent Office by a patent agent whose licence is not suspended or by another person;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 118(4) of the other Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 250(3) of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 250(3) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 12(1)(j.01) of the Patent Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an applicant, patentee or other person may or must be represented in business before the Patent Office by a patent agent whose licence is not suspended or by another person;

Subsection.Text: If section 251 of this Act comes into force before section 119 of the other Act, then that section 119 is repealed.
If section 119 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 251 of this Act, then that section 119 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
If section 139 of the other Act comes into force before section 253 of this Act, then that section 253 is repealed.
If section 139 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 253 of this Act, then that section 253 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: first Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 2. (première loi)
second Act means the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1. (deuxième loi)

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both section 139 of the first Act and section 251 of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 78.22(b) of the Patent Act is amended by striking out the reference to “15” and by making any grammatical changes that the circumstances require; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 64 of the second Act, if it is not in force on that day, is amended by striking out the reference to “15” in the paragraph 78.22(b) that it enacts and by making any grammatical changes to that paragraph that the circumstances require.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (2) produces its effects on the day on which section 64 of the second Act comes into force, then that section 64 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection produces its effects.
On the first day on which section 139 of the first Act is in force and either section 205 of this Act is in force or subsection 210(6) of this Act has produced its effects,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 78.22(b) of the Patent Act is amended by striking out the reference to “15.1” and by making any grammatical changes that the circumstances require; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 64 of the second Act, if it is not in force on that day, is amended by striking out the reference to “15.1” in the paragraph 78.22(b) that it enacts and by making any grammatical changes to that paragraph that the circumstances require.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (4) produces its effects on the day on which section 64 of the second Act comes into force, then that section 64 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection produces its effects.
In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1.
If section 255 of this Act comes into force before section 338 of the other Act, then that section 338 is repealed.
If section 338 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 255 of this Act, then that section 338 is deemed to have come into force before that section 255.
If section 357 of the other Act comes into force before subsection 258(1) of this Act, then that subsection 258(1) is replaced by the following:
Paragraph 65(f) of the Act is repealed.
If subsection 258(1) of this Act comes into force before section 357 of the other Act, then that section 357 is amended by repealing the paragraph 65(f) that it enacts.

If section 357 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 258(1) of this Act, then that section 357 is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 258(1) and subsection (4) applies as a consequence.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: first Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1. (première loi)
second Act means the Combating Counterfeit Products Act. (deuxième loi)

Subsection.Text: If section 357 of the first Act comes into force on or before the day on which subsection 258(2) of this Act comes into force, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 258(2) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 65(l) of the English version of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting communications between the Registrar and any other person;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 50(3) of the second Act comes into force on or before the day on which subsection 258(2) of this Act comes into force and section 357 of the first Act is not in force on that day, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 258(2) is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 65 of the Trade-marks Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (e), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: communications between the Registrar and any other person.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which section 357 of the first Act comes into force, paragraph 65(l) of the English version of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting communications between the Registrar and any other person;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 258(2) of this Act comes into force before section 357 of the first Act and subsection 50(3) of the second Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that subsection 50(3) is repealed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 367(85) and (86) of the first Act are repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which that section 357 comes into force, paragraph 65(l) of the English version of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: respecting communications between the Registrar and any other person;

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1.
If subsection 70(7) of the other Act produces its effects before section 255 of this Act comes into force, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 70(1)(a) and (b) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act as they read immediately before the day on which section 342 of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 comes into force, other than subsections 6(2) to (4), sections 28 and 36, subsections 38(6) to (8) and sections 39, 40 and 66;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Nice Classification in section 2, subsections 6(2) to (4), sections 28 and 36, subsections 38(6) to (12), sections 39 and 40 and subsections 48(3) and (5), as enacted by the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which that section 255 comes into force, paragraph 70(1)(b) of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Nice Classification in section 2, subsections 6(2) to (4), section 36, subsections 38(6) to (12), sections 39 and 40 and subsections 48(3) and (5), as enacted by the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1;

Subsection.Text: If section 255 of this Act comes into force before subsection 70(7) of the other Act produces its effects, then, on the day on which that subsection 70(7) produces its effects, paragraphs 70(1)(a) and (b) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act as they read immediately before the day on which section 342 of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 comes into force, other than subsections 6(2) to (4), section 36, subsections 38(6) to (8) and sections 39, 40 and 66;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Nice Classification in section 2, subsections 6(2) to (4), section 36, subsections 38(6) to (12), sections 39 and 40 and subsections 48(3) and (5), as enacted by the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 70(7) of the other Act produces its effects on the same day as section 255 of this Act comes into force, then paragraphs 70(1)(a) and (b) of the Trade-marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act as they read immediately before the day on which section 342 of the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1 comes into force, other than subsections 6(2) to (4), sections 28 and 36, subsections 38(6) to (8) and sections 39, 40 and 66;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Nice Classification in section 2, subsections 6(2) to (4), section 36, subsections 38(6) to (12), sections 39 and 40 and subsections 48(3) and (5), as enacted by the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1;

Section.Text: The following provisions come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Board in section 2, sections 11 to 20, 25 to 32, 34 to 62 and 64 to 74 and paragraphs 76(1)(c) to (f), (h) and (i) of the College of Patent Agents and Trade-mark Agents Act, as enacted by section 247; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 249, subsections 250(1) and (3), sections 251 to 253 and 255 to 257 and subsection 258(1).

Heading.Label: SUBDIVISION E
Heading.TitleText: Amendments relating to preservation of usage rights

Section.Text: Subsection 65.11(7) of the French version of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Si le débiteur a autorisé par contrat une personne à utiliser un droit de propriété intellectuelle, la résiliation n’empêche pas la personne de l’utiliser ni d’en faire respecter l’utilisation exclusive, à condition qu’elle respecte ses obligations contractuelles à l’égard de l’utilisation de ce droit, et ce, pour la période prévue au contrat et pour toute prolongation de celle-ci dont elle se prévaut de plein droit.

Section.Text: Section 65.13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection.Text: If, on the day on which a notice of intention is filed under section 50.4 or a copy of the proposal is filed under subsection 62(1), the insolvent person is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property that is included in a sale or disposition authorized under subsection (7), that sale or disposition does not affect the other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 72:

Subsection.Text: If the bankrupt is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property that is included in a sale or disposition by the trustee, that sale or disposition does not affect that other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

If the bankrupt is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property, the disclaimer or resiliation of that agreement by the trustee does not affect that other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 246:

Subsection.Text: If the insolvent person or the bankrupt is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property that is included in a sale or disposition by the receiver, that sale or disposition does not affect that other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

If the insolvent person or the bankrupt is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property, the disclaimer or resiliation of that agreement by the receiver does not affect that other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

Section.Text: Section 36 of the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

Subsection.Text: If, on the day on which an order is made under this Act in respect of the company, the company is a party to an agreement that grants to another party a right to use intellectual property that is included in a sale or disposition authorized under subsection (6), that sale or disposition does not affect that other party’s right to use the intellectual property — including the other party’s right to enforce an exclusive use — during the term of the agreement, including any period for which the other party extends the agreement as of right, as long as the other party continues to perform its obligations under the agreement in relation to the use of the intellectual property.

Section.Text: Subsections 65.11(7) and 65.13(9) and sections 72.1 and 246.1 of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, as enacted by sections 265 to 268, apply only in respect of proceedings that are commenced under that Act on or after the day on which this section comes into force.

Subsection 36(8) of the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act, as enacted by section 269, applies only in respect of proceedings that are commenced under that Act on or after the day on which this section comes into force.
This Subdivision comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following after section 23:

The head of a government institution may refuse to disclose any record requested under this Act that contains information that is subject to the privilege set out in section 16.1 of the Patent Act or section 51.13 of the Trade-marks Act.
The Privacy Act is amended by adding the following after section 27:

The head of a government institution may refuse to disclose any personal information requested under subsection 12(1) that is subject to the privilege set out in section 16.1 of the Patent Act or section 51.13 of the Trade-marks Act.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendment to the Pest Control Products Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 42(2)(g) of the Pest Control Products Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any advice from a person or body referred to in paragraph 44(1)(f), unless disclosure of the advice may be refused under section 23 or 23.1 of the Access to Information Act;

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1.

If subsection 366(1) of the other Act comes into force before the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then, on that day, the English version of this Act, other than this section, is amended by replacing “trade-mark”, “trade-marks”, “Trade-mark” and “Trade-marks” with “trademark”, “trademarks”, “Trademark” and “Trademarks”, respectively.

If subsection 366(1) of the other Act comes into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then this Act is deemed to have received royal assent before that subsection 366(1) comes into force.

Section.Text: If Bill C-58, introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Access to Information Act and the Privacy Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 10 of that Act and section 273 of this Act are in force, section 23.1 of the English version of the Access to Information Act is replaced by the following:

The head of a government institution may refuse to disclose any record requested under this Part that contains information that is subject to the privilege set out in section 16.1 of the Patent Act or section 51.13 of the Trade-marks Act.
Subsection 3(2) of the National Research Council Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Council is a corporation that has power to acquire, hold, sell or otherwise dispose of and loan or lease real, personal, movable and immovable property for the purposes of and subject to this Act.
Paragraph 5(1)(l) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: license, sell or otherwise grant or make available to others, and receive royalties, fees and payment for, any intellectual property right — including any patent, copyright, industrial design, trade-mark, trade secret, know-how or other similar right and any such future right that is described under a written agreement — that is held, developed, administered or controlled by the Council, whether vested in Her Majesty in right of Canada or in the Council; and

Subsection.Text: Section 5 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Despite section 9 of the Public Servants Inventions Act, the administration and control of any invention made by a public servant, as defined in section 2 of that Act, who is employed by the Council, and vested in Her Majesty by that Act, and any patent issued with respect to the invention, are vested in the Council.

Section.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition collective society in section 2 of the Copyright Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: carries on the business of collecting and distributing royalties or levies payable under this Act in relation to a repertoire of works, performer’s performances, sound recordings or communication signals of more than one author, performer, sound recording maker or broadcaster; (société de gestion)

Section.Text: Paragraph 19(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a sound recording of a musical work, to the collective society authorized under Part VII.1 to collect them; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 29.9(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sending of information to collective societies that carry on the business of collecting royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3).

Section.Text: Paragraph 30.02(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there is a tariff approved under section 70 that is applicable to the digital reproduction of the work, to the communication of the digital reproduction by telecommunication to persons acting under the authority of the institution and to the printing by those persons of at least one copy of the work; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 30.03(2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of royalties that the institution would have had to pay for the digital reproduction of that work if the tariff had been approved on the day on which the institution first made a digital reproduction under paragraph 30.02(1)(a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of royalties that the institution paid to the society under paragraph 30.02(3)(a) for the digital reproduction of that work from the day on which that paragraph comes into force until the day on which the tariff is approved; and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 30.03(2)(b)(i) and (ii) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of royalties that the institution paid to the society under paragraph 30.02(3)(a) for the digital reproduction of that work from the day on which that paragraph comes into force until the day on which the tariff is approved, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of royalties that the institution would have had to pay for the digital reproduction of that work if the tariff had been approved on the day on which the institution first made a digital reproduction under paragraph 30.02(1)(a).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 30.3(2)(b) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Board has, in accordance with subsection 71(2), fixed the royalty rates and related terms and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a tariff has been approved in accordance with section 70; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a collective society has filed a proposed tariff in accordance with section 68.

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 34(4)(c)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a tariff approved by the Board under Part VII.1 or VIII, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: agreements referred to in subsection 67(3).

Section.Text: Subsection 38.1(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A collective society or copyright owner who has authorized a collective society to act on their behalf may make an election under this section with respect to an act set out in subsection (4.1) only if applicable royalties are set out in an approved tariff or fixed under subsection 71(2) and the defendant has not paid them. If they make the election, the collective society or copyright owner may only recover, in lieu of any other remedy of a monetary nature provided by this Act, an award of statutory damages in respect of such acts in a sum of not less than three and not more than ten times the amount of the applicable royalties, as the court considers just.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Acts for the purposes of subsection (4)
Subsection.Text: Subsection (4) applies with respect to the following acts:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the performance in public of musical works or dramatico-musical works, of performer’s performances of such works, or of sound recordings embodying such works or performances; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the communication to the public by telecommunication of musical works or dramatico-musical works, other than as described in subsection 31(2), of performer’s performances of such works, or of sound recordings embodying such works or performances.

Section.Text: Paragraph 38.2(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under a tariff approved by the Board under section 70.

Section.Text: The headings before section 66 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
There is established a Board to be known as the Copyright Board, consisting of not more than five members, including a Chair and a Vice-chair, to be appointed by the Governor in Council.
Subsection 66(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Chair must be a judge, either sitting or retired, of a superior court.
The portion of subsection 66(7) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A full-time member of the Board, other than the Chair, is deemed to be employed in

Section.Text: Section 66.1 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chair shall direct the work of the Board and apportion its work among its members.
If the Chair is absent or incapacitated or if the office of Chair is vacant, the Vice-chair has all the powers and functions of the Chair during the absence, incapacity or vacancy.
The Vice-chair is the chief executive officer of the Board and has supervision over and direction of the Board and its staff.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 66.5:
The Board shall fix royalty and levy rates and any related terms and conditions under this Act that are fair and equitable, in consideration of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: what would have been agreed upon between a willing buyer and a willing seller acting in a competitive market with all relevant information, at arm’s length and free of external constraints;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the public interest;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any regulation made under subsection 66.91(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other criterion that the Board considers appropriate.

Section.Text: All matters before the Board shall be dealt with as informally and expeditiously as the circumstances and considerations of fairness permit but, in any case, within any period or no later than any day provided for under this Act.
For greater certainty, any person or entity may authorize any other person or entity to act on their behalf in any matter before the Board.

Subsection.Text: The Chair may assign a member, officer or employee of the Board or a person engaged under subsection 66.4(3) to act as a case manager of a matter before the Board.
The case manager may give any directions or make any orders with respect to the case management of the matter, but is not permitted to make a direction or order that is inconsistent with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: regulations made under subsection 66.6(1), unless authorized to do so under regulations made under paragraph 66.6(1.1)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: regulations made under paragraph 66.91(2)(a) to (c), unless authorized to do so under regulations made under paragraph 66.91(2)(d).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed direction or order of Board
Subsection.Text: A direction given, or an order made, by a case manager is deemed to be a direction or order of the Board, including for the purposes of paragraph 28(1)(j) of the Federal Courts Act.
The Chair may delegate his or her power under subsection (1) to the Vice-chair.

Section.Text: Section 66.52 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A decision of the Board respecting royalties or their related terms and conditions that is made under subsection 70(1), 71(2), 76.1(1) or 83(8) may, on application, be varied by the Board if, in its opinion, there has been a material change in circumstances since the decision was made.
Subsection 66.6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Board may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, make regulations governing the case management of matters before the Board, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: governing the directions a case manager may give and the orders they may make; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing a case manager to give a direction or make an order that adapts, restricts or excludes the application of any provision of regulations made under subsection (1) to a matter or any step in a matter.

Subsection.Text: A copy of each regulation that the Board proposes to make under subsection (1) or (1.1) shall be published in the Canada Gazette at least 60 days before the regulation’s proposed effective date, and a reasonable opportunity shall be given to interested persons to make representations with respect to the regulation.

Section.Text: Section 66.91 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 66.91(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the day by which, or the period within which, a matter before the Board — and any procedural step in the matter, whether set out in a provision of this Act or not — must be completed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the minimum length of the effective period for the purposes of subsections 68.1(2) and 83(4);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establishing a day for the purposes of paragraph 73.4(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the Board or a case manager to give a direction or make an order that adapts, restricts or excludes the application of any provision of regulations made under any of paragraphs (a) to (c) to a matter or any step in a matter.

Subsection.Text: Regulations made under subsection (2) prevail over regulations made under subsection 66.6(1) or (1.1) to the extent of an inconsistency or conflict between them.

Section.Text: The heading before section 67 and sections 67 to 76 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A collective society may file a proposed tariff with the Board for the purpose of establishing royalties with respect to rights the collective society administers under section 3, 15, 18, 19 or 21.
However, a collective society shall file a proposed tariff with the Board for the purpose of establishing royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3) or paragraph 31(2)(d).

A collective society may enter into agreements for the purpose of establishing royalties with respect to rights the collective society administers under section 3, 15, 18, 19 or 21, other than royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3) or paragraph 31(2)(d).

Section.Text: On application by a collective society, the Board may designate the collective society as the sole collective society authorized to collect all royalties referred to in paragraph 19(2)(a) with respect to a sound recording of a musical work.

A collective society shall answer, within a reasonable time, all reasonable requests from any person for information about its repertoire of works, performer’s performances, sound recordings or communication signals.

A proposed tariff must be filed no later than October 15 of the second calendar year before the calendar year in which the proposed tariff is to take effect or, if a day is established under regulations made under subsection 66.91(2), no later than that day.

Subsection.Text: A proposed tariff must be filed in both official languages and include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the acts to which the tariff is to apply;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed royalty rates and any related terms and conditions; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the effective period of the proposed tariff.

Subsection.Text: A proposed tariff’s effective period must be at least three calendar years or, if a minimum period is established under regulations made under subsection 66.91(2), at least that minimum period.

Section.Text: The Board, in the manner that it sees fit,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall publish the proposed tariff as well as a notice that any objection to the proposed tariff must be filed within the period set out in subsection 68.3(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may distribute a notice — or cause it to be distributed or published, on any terms and conditions that the Board sees fit — of the publication of the tariff and of the notice referred to in paragraph (a) to any person affected by the proposed tariff.

Subsection.Text: An objection to a proposed tariff may be filed with the Board by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an educational institution, if the proposed tariff is filed for the purpose of collecting royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a retransmitter, as defined in subsection 31(1), if the proposed tariff is filed for the purpose of collecting royalties referred to in paragraph 31(2)(d); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any user, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: An objection shall be filed no later than the 30th day after the day on which the Board published the proposed tariff under paragraph 68.2(a) or, if a day is established under the regulations made under subsection 66.91(2), no later than that day.
The Board shall provide a copy of the filed objection to the collective society.
The collective society may file a reply to an objection with the Board.
The Board shall provide a copy of the filed reply to the person or entity that filed the objection.

Heading.TitleText: Withdrawal or Amendment of Proposed Tariff

Section.Text: A collective society may, before a proposed tariff filed by it has been approved by the Board, make an application to the Board requesting that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed tariff be withdrawn; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to an act set out in the proposed tariff be excluded from the approved tariff for all of the proposed effective period or, despite subsection 68.1(2), for a portion of that period.

Subsection.Text: The Board shall approve an application made under section 69 if it is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the collective society has provided sufficient public notice of its intention to make the application;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every person who, in respect of the proposed effective period, has paid royalties that would not be payable if the application were approved has

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: consented to the application,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: received a refund of the royalties, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: entered into an agreement under subsection 67(3) that covers the act, repertoire or proposed effective period that is the subject of the application; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an application made under paragraph 69(b) with respect to a portion of the proposed effective period, the application is not made for the purpose of improperly circumventing the required minimum effective period.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the approval of an application made under section 69 does not preclude the collective society from filing, in accordance with this Act, a proposed tariff that deals in whole or in part with the act, repertoire or proposed effective period that was the subject of the application.

The Board shall — within the period, if any, that is established under regulations made under subsection 66.91(2) — approve the proposed tariff after making any alterations to the royalty rates and the related terms and conditions, or fixing any new related terms and conditions, that the Board considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Factors — performances of musical works and sound recordings
Subsection.Text: In approving a proposed tariff for the performance in public or the communication to the public by telecommunication of performer’s performances of musical works, or of sound recordings embodying such performer’s performances, the Board shall ensure that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the tariff applies in respect of performer’s performances and sound recordings only in the situations referred to in the provisions of section 20 other than subsections 20(3) and (4);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the tariff does not, because of linguistic and content requirements of Canada’s broadcasting policy set out in section 3 of the Broadcasting Act, place some users that are subject to that Act at a greater financial disadvantage than others; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the payment of royalties by users under section 19 will be made in a single payment.

Subsection.Text: The Board shall fix a preferential royalty rate for small cable transmission systems in approving a tariff for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the performance in public of musical works or dramatico-musical works, of performer’s performances of such works, or of sound recordings embodying such works; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the communication to the public by telecommunication of musical works or dramatico-musical works, other than as described in subsection 31(2), of performer’s performances of such works, or of sound recordings embodying such works.

Subsection.Text: The Board shall fix a preferential royalty rate for small retransmission systems in approving a tariff for royalties referred to in paragraph 31(2)(d).
For greater certainty, the Board may determine, in respect of any tariff that it approves, the portion of the royalties that is to be paid to each collective society.

For greater certainty, the Board must not discriminate between owners of copyright on the ground of their nationality or residence in approving a tariff for royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3) or paragraph 31(2)(d).
The Governor in Council may make regulations defining “small cable transmission system” and “small retransmission system” for the purposes of this section.

Section.Text: The Board shall publish the approved tariff in the Canada Gazette and provide a copy of it, together with the reasons for the Board’s decision, to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the collective society that filed the proposed tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every collective society that is authorized to collect royalties under the tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: every person or entity that filed an objection under section 68.3; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other person or entity that, in the Board’s opinion, ought to receive the copy and reasons.

Heading.TitleText: Fixing of Royalty Rates in Individual Cases

Subsection.Text: If a collective society and a user are unable to agree on royalties to be paid with respect to rights under section 3, 15, 18, 19 or 21, other than royalties referred to in subsection 29.7(2) or (3) or paragraph 31(2)(d), or are unable to agree on any related terms and conditions, the collective society or user may, after giving notice to the other party, apply to the Board to fix the royalty rates or any related terms and conditions, or both.
The Board may, for a period that the Board may specify, fix the royalty rates or their related terms and conditions, or both, as the case may be.
Subsections 70(2) and (3) apply, with any necessary modifications, to the fixing of royalty rates or terms and conditions, or both, by the Board under subsection (2).
For greater certainty, the Board may deny an application made under subsection (1) or any part of one.
The Board shall send a copy of the decision and the reasons for it to the collective society and the user.
In this section, user means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a user who is not otherwise authorized to do an act referred to in section 3, 15, 18 or 21 in respect of the works, performer’s performances, sound recordings or communication signals included in a collective society’s repertoire; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a user who is required to pay, in respect of sound recordings included in a collective society’s repertoire, a royalty referred to in section 19 that has not otherwise been fixed or agreed on.

Section.Text: The Board shall not proceed with an application under section 71 in respect of any matter in issue on which an agreement has been reached, if a notice is filed with the Board that such an agreement has been reached.

Heading.TitleText: Special Rules Related to Royalty Rates

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) and (3) apply despite the tariffs approved by the Board under section 70, or despite the royalty rates fixed under subsection 71(2), for the performance in public or the communication to the public by telecommunication of performer’s performances of musical works or of sound recordings embodying such performer’s performances.
For wireless transmission systems, other than community systems and public transmission systems, broadcasters shall pay

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $100 on the first $1.25 million of annual advertising revenues in respect of each year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
