The second paragraph of the preamble to the English version of the First Nations Land Management Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: WHEREAS the ratification of the Agreement by Her Majesty requires the enactment of an Act of Parliament;

Subsection.Text: The preamble to the Act is amended by adding the following after the second paragraph:

Provision.Text: AND WHEREAS the Government of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples;

Subsection.Text: The definitions First Nation and First Nation land in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: First Nation means a band named in Schedule 1. (première nation)

First Nation land means reserve land or lands set aside to which a land code applies. It includes all the interests or rights in, and resources of, the land that are within the legislative authority of Parliament. (terres de la première nation)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: lands set aside means land in Yukon that is reserved or set aside by notation in the property records of the Department of Indian Affairs and Northern Development for the use of Indigenous peoples in Yukon. (terres mises de côté)

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 5(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reserve land to which a land code applies continues to be set apart for the use and benefit of the First Nation for which it was set apart; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reserve land to which a land code applies continues to be land reserved for the Indians within the meaning of Class 24 of section 91 of the Constitution Act, 1867.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Section.Text: For greater certainty, lands set aside to which a land code applies are not a reserve.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A First Nation that wishes to establish a land management regime in accordance with the Framework Agreement and this Act shall adopt a land code applicable to all land in a reserve of, or in a parcel of lands set aside for, the First Nation and that land code shall include the following matters:
Paragraph 6(1)(f) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 6(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (l) and by replacing paragraph (m) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the land code will come into force, which may be the day on which the validity of the land code is certified or a later day that is within six months after that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the procedures for amending the land code.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For greater certainty, a First Nation may establish a land management regime for any or all of its reserves or parcels of lands set aside.

Section.Text: Section 6.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Reserve set apart for more than one First Nation

Subsection.Text: All of the First Nations for whose use and benefit a reserve has been set apart may, in accordance with the Framework Agreement and this Act, establish a land management regime applicable to all land in the reserve if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each of those First Nations has a land code in force;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each of those First Nations amends their land code to include

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the land that the Surveyor General may prepare or cause to be prepared or any other description that is, in the Surveyor General’s opinion, sufficient to identify the land,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: uniform rules and procedures for the management of the land, including the matters described in paragraphs 6(1)(b) to (l), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: uniform rules and procedures for the resolution of disputes between the First Nations concerning the management of the land; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the individual agreements between each of those First Nations and the Minister are amended to include a description of the land in the reserve and to provide for the matters described in paragraphs 6(3)(a) to (d) in relation to that land.

Subsection.Text: The amendments to the land codes come into force on the first day on which all of the amended individual agreements have been signed by the First Nations and the Minister or on a later date that is agreed to by all of the First Nations and the Minister.
The land codes of the First Nations referred to in subsection (1) shall, to the extent that the land codes apply to the reserve referred to in that subsection, remain uniform.

Any powers, duties or functions that may be exercised or performed under this Act by a First Nation or its council in relation to a reserve referred to in subsection (1) shall be exercised or performed jointly, in accordance with their land codes, by all of the First Nations for whose use and benefit the reserve has been set apart, by their councils or by a person or body to whom a power, duty or function is delegated by those First Nations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coming into force of land code
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of section 16, subsection 31(2) and sections 34, 38 and 39, as they apply in relation to a reserve referred to in subsection (1), the coming into force of the land code is deemed to be the day on which the amendments to the land codes come into force in accordance with subsection (2).

Section.Text: If the Surveyor General prepares or causes to be prepared a description of lands under paragraph 6(1)(a) or 6.01(1)(b), the Surveyor General may, if he or she considers it appropriate, survey or have those lands surveyed in accordance with the Canada Lands Surveys Act.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 7(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Despite subsections 6(1) and 6.01(1), a portion of a reserve or of a parcel of lands set aside may be excluded from the application of a land code if
Subsection 7(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A portion of a reserve or of a parcel of lands set aside shall not be excluded from a land code if the exclusion would have the effect of placing the administration of a lease, other interest or a right in that land in more than one land management regime.
Subsection 7.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections 6(1) and 6.01(1), land may be excluded from the application of a land code if it is uncertain whether the land is located within the boundaries of the reserve or of the parcel of lands set aside.
Subsection 7.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The exclusion of the land does not preclude the First Nation or Her Majesty from asserting in an action, a lawsuit or other proceeding that the land is part of the reserve or of the parcel of lands set aside.

Section.Text: Section 7.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A First Nation shall amend the description of First Nation land in its land code to include a portion of a reserve or of a parcel of lands set aside that was excluded under subsection 7(1) or land that was excluded under subsection 7.1(1) if the First Nation and the Minister agree that the condition that justified the exclusion no longer exists, and the individual agreement shall be amended accordingly.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: determine whether the conduct of a community approval process is in accordance with the process confirmed under paragraph (a), unless a ratification officer is appointed under section 8.1; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The verifier shall determine any dispute arising between a First Nation and the Minister before a land code comes into force regarding the terms of the transfer of administration of land or the exclusion of a portion of a reserve or of a parcel of lands set aside from the application of a land code.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:
A First Nation may appoint a ratification officer who shall determine whether the conduct of a community approval process is in accordance with the process confirmed under paragraph 8(1)(a).
Subsections 10(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who is 18 years of age or over and who is a First Nation member, whether or not resident on the land that is subject to the proposed land code, is eligible to vote in the community approval process.

The council shall, before proceeding to obtain community approval, take reasonable measures, such as those described in the Framework Agreement, to locate voters and inform them of their right to vote, the means of exercising that right and the content of the Framework Agreement, this Act, any resolutions made under subsection 12(2), the proposed land code and the individual agreement.
The council may conduct a vote by electronic means.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a ratification officer is appointed under section 8.1, he or she shall perform the duties and functions of the verifier that are set out in section 11. The ratification officer shall also send the report referred to in subsection 11(3) to the verifier.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), a proposed land code and an individual agreement that have been submitted for community approval are approved if a majority of eligible voters who participated in the vote voted to approve them.
A council may, by resolution,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fix the minimum percentage of eligible voters who are required to participate in a vote in order for the proposed land code and individual agreement to be approved; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fix an approval rate that is greater than what is required by subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 13(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: without delay, a copy of the approved code; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 13(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An eligible voter may, within five days after the conclusion of a vote, report any irregularity in the voting process to the verifier.
The portion of subsection 14(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The verifier shall, after receiving the documents referred to in subsection 13(1), certify the validity of the land code unless the verifier, after giving the First Nation a reasonable opportunity to make submissions on the matter but within 10 days after the conclusion of the vote, is of the opinion that
Section 14 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If a ratification officer is appointed under section 8.1, the verifier shall certify the validity of the land code only after receiving the ratification officer’s report under section 11.1. The 10-day period referred to in subsection (1) begins after the day on which the verifier receives that report.
Subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (1.1), a land code comes into force and has the force of law on the day that is specified in the land code, and judicial notice shall be taken of the land code in any proceedings from the date of the coming into force of that land code.
Subsection 15(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Without delay after the coming into force of the land code of a First Nation or of any amendment to the land code, the First Nation shall publish the land code on its website, if it has one. The First Nation shall also make a copy of it available, on request, to any person.

Section.Text: Section 16 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: The terms of any designations made under the Indian Act before the coming into force of a land code do not restrict the First Nation and the holder of an interest or right in or a licence in relation to First Nation land from modifying that interest, right or licence after the coming into force of the land code.

Section.Text: Section 17 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 19 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 46.1, on the coming into force of the land code of a First Nation, all revenue moneys and capital moneys held by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation or its First Nation members cease to be Indian moneys and shall be transferred to the First Nation. All revenue moneys and capital moneys collected or received by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation or its First Nation members after the transfer are not Indian moneys and shall be transferred to the First Nation.

Her Majesty is not liable in respect of anything done or omitted to be done in relation to the management of revenue moneys or capital moneys transferred to a First Nation under this Act by the First Nation or any person or body authorized by the First Nation to act in relation to those moneys.
Subsection 20(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by replacing paragraph (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the rules and procedures that apply, during a conjugal relationship, when that relationship breaks down or on the death of a spouse or common-law partner, with respect to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the use, occupation and possession of family homes on First Nation land,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the division of the value of any interests or rights held by spouses or common-law partners in or to First Nation land or structures on First Nation land, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period of cohabitation in a conjugal relationship required for an individual to be a common-law partner;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: limits on the liability of, and defences and immunities for, any person or body in respect of an act or omission occurring in the exercise of a power or the performance of a duty or function under a First Nation law or the land code; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any matter arising out of or ancillary to the exercise of the power to enact laws under paragraphs (a) to (d).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A First Nation law enacted under paragraph (1)(c) may include, despite subsection 89(1) of the Indian Act, provisions for enforcing, on First Nation land, an order of a court that includes one or more provisions made under the law or a decision made or an agreement reached under the law.

If the council of a First Nation intends to enact a First Nation law under paragraph (1)(c), the council shall so notify the Attorney General of the province in which the First Nation land is situated. If the First Nation law is enacted, the council shall, without delay, send a copy to that Attorney General.

MarginalNote.XRefExternal: Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act

Subsection.Text: In the event of any inconsistency or conflict between the provisions of a First Nation law enacted under paragraph (1)(c) and the provisions of a law enacted under section 7 of the Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act, the provisions of the First Nation law enacted under paragraph (1)(c) prevail to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.

A First Nation law enacted under paragraph (1)(d) shall not provide for greater limits on liability, defences and immunities than those that would apply to a person or body exercising a similar power or performing a similar duty or function under the laws of the province in which the First Nation land is situated.

A First Nation law may provide for enforcement measures, consistent with federal laws or the laws of the province in which the First Nation land is situated, such as the power to inspect, search and seize and to order compulsory sampling, testing and the production of information.

If a First Nation has, under another Act of Parliament, enacted laws or made by-laws respecting the enforcement of laws or by-laws respecting the taxation for local purposes of reserve lands, interests or rights in reserve lands or rights to occupy, possess or use reserve lands, the First Nation may use the enforcement measures provided for in those laws or by-laws to enforce the payment of any amount payable to the First Nation under its First Nation laws or its land code.
Subsection (3.1) does not apply to the enforcement of the payment of any amount payable to a First Nation under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a First Nation law that applies to a reserve referred to in subsection 6.01(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the land code of a First Nation to the extent that it applies to a reserve referred to in subsection 6.01(1).

Subsection.Text: A First Nation may enter into an agreement with a government or government agency respecting the collection of any amount payable to the First Nation under its First Nation laws or its land code.
Section 20 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: family home means a structure — that need not be affixed but that must be situated on First Nation land — where the spouses or common-law partners habitually reside or, if they have ceased to cohabit or one of them has died, where they habitually resided on the day on which they ceased to cohabit or the death occurred. If the structure is normally used for a purpose in addition to a residential purpose, this definition includes only the portion of the structure that may reasonably be regarded as necessary for the residential purpose. (foyer familial)
spouse has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act. (époux)

Section.Text: Paragraph 22(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: enter into an agreement with a provincial government for the use of provincial prosecutors; or

Section.Text: Section 23 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In any proceedings, a copy of a First Nation law appearing to be certified as a true copy by an officer of the First Nation — or, in the case of a First Nation law that applies to First Nation land that is a reserve referred to in subsection 6.01(1), by an officer of any of the First Nations for whose use and benefit the reserve has been set apart — is, without proof of the officer’s signature or official character, evidence of its enactment on the date specified in the law.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 25(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 25(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer of the administration of the Register to any person or body and the disclosure of personal information as defined in section 3 of the Privacy Act for that purpose.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 25:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, at the request of a First Nation that has a land code in force, set apart as a reserve for the use and benefit of that First Nation any lands the title to which is vested in Her Majesty or for which Her Majesty has the administration and control.

The lands that are the subject of the order become First Nation land on the day on which the order is made. The land code of the First Nation and the individual agreement between the First Nation and the Minister are deemed, on that day, to be amended to include a description of those lands.
Before the order is made, the First Nation may, in accordance with its land code,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: grant interests or rights in and licences in relation to the lands that are subject to the request referred to in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: enact laws respecting any matters referred to in section 20 that will apply to those lands.

Subsection.Text: Any interests, rights or licences granted by the First Nation under subsection (3) take effect on the day on which the order is made and any laws enacted by the First Nation under that subsection come into force on that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Copy of order in First Nation Land Register
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall record a copy of the order in the First Nation Land Register.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coming into force of land code
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of sections 16, 34 and 38, as those sections apply in relation to First Nation land that was the subject of the order, the coming into force of the land code is deemed to be the day on which the order is made.

A First Nation is not liable in respect of anything done or omitted to be done, before the day on which an order is made under subsection 25.1(1), by Her Majesty or any person or body authorized by Her Majesty to act in relation to the granting of interests or rights in or licences in relation to the lands that are the subject of the order or the obtaining of discharges of any of those interests, rights or licences.
Her Majesty shall indemnify a First Nation for any loss suffered by the First Nation as a result of an act or omission described in subsection (1).

Her Majesty is not liable in respect of anything done or omitted to be done under paragraph 25.1(3)(a) by the First Nation or any person or body authorized by the First Nation to act under that paragraph.
The First Nation shall indemnify Her Majesty for any loss suffered by Her Majesty as a result of an act or omission described in subsection (3).

Section.Text: Paragraph 27(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: compensation for the First Nation land includes land that Her Majesty has agreed will be set apart as a reserve or set aside as a parcel of lands set aside and that is to become First Nation land; and

Section.Text: Subsection 31(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Land provided to a First Nation as compensation may be of an area that is less than the area of the land in which an interest or right has been expropriated if the total area of the land comprised in a reserve of or in the lands set aside for the First Nation is not less following the expropriation than at the coming into force of its land code.

Section.Text: Sections 35 and 36 of the Act are replaced by the following:

No criminal or civil proceedings lie against an arbitrator, mediator, neutral evaluator, verifier or ratification officer appointed under the Framework Agreement or this Act or any member of a board established by section 38 of the Framework Agreement who is, in good faith, exercising a power or performing a duty or function in accordance with the Framework Agreement or this Act for anything done or omitted to be done during the course of the exercise or purported exercise of any power or the performance or purported performance of any duty or function of that person in accordance with the Framework Agreement or this Act.

Subsection.Text: Every determination under this Act or the Framework Agreement by a verifier, ratification officer or arbitrator is final, and no order shall be made, process entered or proceedings taken in any court, whether by way of injunction, certiorari, prohibition, mandamus, quo warranto or otherwise, to question, review or prohibit such a determination.

No order shall be made, process entered or proceedings taken in any court, whether by way of injunction, certiorari, prohibition, mandamus, quo warranto or otherwise, to question, review or prohibit any other action under this Act or the Framework Agreement by a verifier, ratification officer or arbitrator or any action under the Framework Agreement by a neutral evaluator.

Despite subsections (1) and (2), the Attorney General of Canada or anyone directly affected by the matter in respect of which relief is sought may make an application under the Federal Courts Act on any of the grounds referred to in paragraph 18.1(4)(a) or (b) of that Act for any relief against a verifier, ratification officer, arbitrator or neutral evaluator by way of an injunction or declaration or by way of an order in the nature of certiorari, prohibition, mandamus or quo warranto.
Subsection 38(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: sections 61 to 65, 67 and 68 of that Act, other than in respect of moneys collected, received or held by Her Majesty under that Act for the use and benefit of an individual;

Subsection.Text: Section 38 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If the individual agreement between a First Nation and the Minister is amended to provide for the transfer of capital moneys in accordance with subsection 46.1(1), then sections 61 to 65, 67 and 68 of the Indian Act cease to apply to the First Nation, First Nation members and First Nation land, other than in respect of moneys collected, received or held by Her Majesty under that Act for the use and benefit of an individual.
Subsection 38(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A land code may extend the application of subsection 89(1.1) of the Indian Act, or any portion of it, to other leasehold interests or leases in reserve land to which a land code applies.
Subsection 39(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Indian Oil and Gas Act

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: continues to apply in respect of any reserve land to which a land code applies that was subject to that Act on the coming into force of the land code; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: applies in respect of an interest or right in reserve land to which a land code applies that is granted to Her Majesty for the exploitation of oil and gas under the land code.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

For greater certainty, the provisions of the Indian Oil and Gas Act respecting the payment of royalties to Her Majesty in trust for a First Nation apply, despite any other provision of this Act, in respect of reserve land referred to in subsection (1).

Section.Text: Section 45 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, add the name of a band to Schedule 1 if he or she is satisfied that the signing of the Framework Agreement on the band’s behalf has been duly authorized and that the Framework Agreement has been signed.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coming into force of land code
Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, add to Schedule 2 the name of a First Nation that has a land code in force and the date on which the land code comes into force with respect to First Nation land.
The Minister may, by order, amend Schedule 1 or 2 to amend the name of a First Nation.

The Minister may, by order, delete from Schedule 1 or 2 the name of a First Nation and delete from Schedule 2 the date on which a land code comes into force with respect to the First Nation’s lands, if that First Nation is no longer subject to this Act under the terms of a land claims agreement or a self-government agreement.

Section.Text: The heading before section 46 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The heading after section 46 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a First Nation’s land code comes into force before the day on which this section comes into force, all capital moneys held by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation or its First Nation members cease to be Indian moneys and shall be transferred to the First Nation if the individual agreement between the First Nation and the Minister is amended to provide for it. All capital moneys collected or received by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation or its First Nation members after the transfer are not Indian moneys and shall be transferred to the First Nation.

The council of the First Nation shall, at least 30 days before the individual agreement is amended, inform the First Nation members of the council’s intention to amend the individual agreement and of the amount of capital moneys held by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation or its First Nation members.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is replaced by the Schedules 1 and 2 set out in Schedule 2 to this Act.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 12(2)(b) of the Family Homes on Reserves and Matrimonial Interests or Rights Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the First Nation laws that it enacts under section 7 of this Act or paragraph 20(1)(c) of that Act are not in force.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the First Nations Fiscal Management Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: capital assets includes capital infrastructure. (immobilisation)

interest, in relation to reserve lands in Canada elsewhere than in Quebec, means any estate, right or interest of any nature in or to the lands, including any right to occupy, possess or use the lands, but does not include title to the lands that is held by her Majesty. (intérêt)

right, in relation to reserve lands in Quebec, means any right of any nature in or to the lands, including any right to occupy, possess or use the lands and any right of a lessee, but does not include title to the lands that is held by her Majesty. (droit)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For greater certainty, nothing in this Act shall be construed as requiring capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands to be located on reserve lands.
The portion of subsection 5(1) of the Act before subparagraph (a)(ii) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsections (2) to (5), sections 4 and 6 and any regulations made under paragraph 36(1)(d), the council of a First Nation may make laws

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting taxation for local purposes of reserve lands and interests or rights in reserve lands, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the assessment of the value of those lands and interests or rights, the requisition of any information necessary to conduct the assessment and the inspection, in accordance with procedures prescribed by regulation, for assessment purposes of any reserve lands that are subject to taxation for local purposes,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 5(1)(a)(ii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a mechanism to establish tax rates and apply them to the assessed value of those lands and interests or rights,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 5(1)(e)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the creation of liens or, in Quebec, prior claims or legal hypothecs on reserve lands and on interests or rights in reserve lands,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 5(1)(e)(iii) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: sous réserve du paragraphe (7), la saisie, la confiscation et la cession de droits ou intérêts sur les terres de réserve,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 5(1)(e)(iv) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the seizure and sale of personal or movable property located on reserve lands, other than property located in a dwelling,

Subsection.Text: Subsections 5(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Despite the Indian Act or any instrument or act conferring an interest or right in reserve lands, if there are outstanding taxes payable under a law made under paragraph (1)(a) for more than two years, the First Nation may assign the interest or right in accordance with the conditions and procedures prescribed by regulation.

Section.Text: The portion of section 7 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

When the council of a First Nation sends a law made under paragraph 5(1)(a), (a.1) or (c), other than a law referred to in subsection 10(1), to the First Nations Tax Commission for its approval, the council shall
Paragraphs 8(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the lands and interests or rights subject to the law;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the assessment practices to be applied to each class of land and interest or right;

Section.Text: Subsection 10(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A council of a First Nation that makes a property taxation law that requires a rate of tax to be set annually shall also make a law under paragraph 5(1)(a) setting the rate of tax to be applied to the assessed value of each class of lands and interests or rights at least once each year on or before the date prescribed by regulation or, if none is so prescribed, on or before the date fixed by standards established under subsection 35(1).

Section.Text: Paragraph 14(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other persons who have an interest or right in the First Nation’s reserve lands;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 17(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
La Commission a la capacité d’une personne physique et les droits, pouvoirs et privilèges de celle-ci; elle peut notamment :
Paragraph 17(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquire, hold and dispose of property or an interest or right in property, or lease property;

Section.Text: Subsection 26(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The head office of the Commission shall be on the reserve lands of the Tk’emlúps te Secwépemc or at any other location that the Governor in Council determines.

Section.Text: Subsection 31(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Before approving a law made under paragraph 5(1)(a), (a.1) or (c), other than a law referred to in subsection 10(1), the Commission shall consider, in accordance with any regulations made under paragraph 36(1)(b), any representations made to it under paragraph 7(b) in respect of the law.
The portion of subsection 32(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Commission shall not approve a law made under paragraph 5(1)(d) for financing of capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands unless
The portion of subsection 32(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

On approving a law made by a First Nation under paragraph 5(1)(d) for financing of capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands, the Commission shall provide the First Nations Finance Authority with
Subsection 32(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Commission becomes aware that judicial review proceedings have been undertaken in respect of a law made by a First Nation under paragraph 5(1)(d) for financing of capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands, the Commission shall without delay inform the First Nations Finance Authority of those proceedings.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 33(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On the request in writing by a member of a First Nation, or by a person who holds an interest or right in reserve lands, who
Paragraph 35(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: criteria for the approval of laws made under paragraph 5(1)(d) or (f);

Paragraph.Label: (c.01)
Paragraph.Text: criteria for the approval of local revenue laws respecting reserve lands that have been set apart for the use and benefit of more than one First Nation, including criteria in relation to the First Nations entering into agreements in relation to the administration of those local revenue laws and criteria in respect of those agreements;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 35(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: resolution of disputes with First Nations concerning the taxation of interests or rights on reserve lands.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 38(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Le Conseil a la capacité d’une personne physique et les droits, pouvoirs et privilèges de celle-ci; il peut notamment :
Paragraph 38(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquire, hold and dispose of property or an interest or right in property, or lease property;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 50:

Section.MarginalNote: Review of financial management system — non-scheduled entities

Subsection.Text: On the request of any of the following entities, the Board may review the entity’s financial management system, financial performance or laws or by-laws respecting financial administration to determine whether it is in compliance, in all material respects, with the standards established under subsection (3):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a band that is not named in the schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a tribal council;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an Aboriginal group that is a party to a treaty, land claims agreement or self-government agreement with Canada or with a province, or an entity established under, or as a result of, such a treaty or agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an entity — owned or controlled by one or more First Nations or entities referred to in paragraphs (a), (b) or (c) — whose mandate is primarily to promote the well-being or advancement of Aboriginal people; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a not-for-profit organization established to provide public services, including social welfare, housing, recreational, cultural, health or educational services, to Aboriginal groups or Aboriginal persons.

Subsection.Text: On completion of a review under subsection (1), the Board shall provide to the entity a report setting out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the scope of the review undertaken; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an opinion as to whether the entity was in compliance, in all material respects, with the standards or as to which aspects of the standards were not complied with by the entity.

Subsection.Text: The Board may establish standards, not inconsistent with the regulations, respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: financial management systems and financial performance of entities referred to in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the form and content of laws or by-laws respecting financial administration of entities referred to in subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: The Board may establish procedures respecting the review referred to in subsection (1).
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to a standard established under subsection (3) or a procedure established under subsection (4).
All standards established by the Board under subsection (3) shall be published in the First Nations Gazette.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 53(2)(b) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: act in the place of the council of the First Nation to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: exercise any powers and fulfil any obligations of the council under this Act, the regulations made under this Act or any laws made under paragraphs 5(1)(a) to (e),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: manage the First Nation’s local revenues, including the local revenue account,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: undertake any necessary borrowing for the purpose of remedying the situation for which third-party management was required, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: provide for the delivery of programs and services that are paid for out of local revenues, manage assets related to those programs and services and enter into or terminate agreements in respect of those programs, services and assets;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: assign interests or rights under subsection 5(7); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: exercise any powers or fulfil any obligations delegated to the Board under a law of the First Nation or an agreement between the First Nation and the Board or between the First Nation and the First Nations Finance Authority.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 56:

For the purpose of enabling an entity referred to in any of paragraphs 50.1(1)(a) to (e) to obtain the services of the Board, other than co-management and third-party management services, the Governor in Council may make any regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adapting any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: restricting the application of any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act.

Section.Text: Subsections 61(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Authority shall be managed by a board of directors, consisting of from 5 to 11 directors, including a Chairperson and Deputy Chairperson, elected from among the representatives of borrowing members.

A representative of a borrowing member may nominate a representative of a borrowing member for election as Chairperson or Deputy Chairperson or as a director other than the Chairperson or Deputy Chairperson.
Paragraph 63(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person ceases to hold office as a chief or councillor of a First Nation that is a borrowing member;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 63(3)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person’s designation as a representative of a borrowing member is revoked by a resolution of the council of that First Nation; or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 74(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: long-term financing or lease financing of capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 74(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide investment services to First Nations and entities referred to in any of paragraphs 50.1(1)(a) to (e); and

Section.Text: Subsection 78(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: L’Administration a priorité sur tous les autres créanciers d’une première nation insolvable pour les sommes dont le versement à l’Administration est autorisé par un texte législatif pris en vertu des alinéas 5(1)b) ou d), par un accord régissant un compte de recettes garanties en fiducie ou en fidéicommis ou par la présente loi, en ce qui concerne toute créance qui prend naissance à la date à laquelle la première nation reçoit le versement initial du premier prêt qu’elle a obtenu auprès de l’Administration ou après cette date.

Section.Text: Section 79 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Authority shall not make a long-term loan to a borrowing member for the purpose of financing capital assets for the provision of local services on reserve lands unless the First Nations Tax Commission has approved a law made by the borrowing member under paragraph 5(1)(d).
Paragraphs 84(5)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by less than 50% of the total amount contributed by borrowing members who have obtained financing for which that debt reserve fund was established, the Authority may, in accordance with the regulations, require those borrowing members to pay without delay amounts sufficient to replenish the debt reserve fund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by 50% or more of the total amount contributed by borrowing members who have obtained financing for which that debt reserve fund was established, the Authority shall, in accordance with the regulations, require those borrowing members to pay without delay amounts sufficient to replenish the debt reserve fund.

Section.Text: Section 89 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (d).
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 89:

Subsection.Text: The council of a First Nation may submit to the Minister a resolution of the council requesting the payment to the First Nation of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: moneys held by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: moneys to be collected or received in future by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation.

Subsection.Text: The resolution shall be accompanied by proof that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the council has made a law respecting the financial administration of the First Nation under paragraph 9(1)(a) and the law has been approved by the First Nations Financial Management Board;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the council has obtained independent legal advice and independent financial advice with respect to the risks of the payment of the moneys to the First Nation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the payment of the moneys to the First Nation has been approved under section 91.

Subsection.Text: If the council of a First Nation intends to request the payment of moneys under subsection 90(1), the council shall conduct a vote among the First Nation’s eligible voters on the approval of the payment of the moneys.
Every First Nation member, whether resident on a reserve of the First Nation or not, is an eligible voter if the member is at least 18 years old on the date of the vote.

Before conducting the vote under subsection (1), the council shall obtain independent legal advice and independent financial advice with respect to the risks of the payment of the moneys to the First Nation.
Before conducting the vote under subsection (1), the council shall also take reasonable measures that are in accordance with the First Nation’s practices to inform eligible voters of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: their right to vote and the means of exercising that right;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that council has obtained the legal and financial advice referred to in subsection (3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the implications of the payment of the moneys and the reasons why the payment is for the benefit of the First Nation; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that the council has made a law respecting the financial administration of the First Nation under paragraph 9(1)(a).

Subsection.Text: The payment of the moneys to the First Nation is approved if a majority of eligible voters who participated voted to approve the payment.
Despite subsection (5), the payment is not approved unless at least 25% of all eligible voters participated in the vote.
The council may, by resolution adopted before the vote, increase the percentage of eligible voters required under subsection (6).

After the resolution is submitted to the Minister by the council of the First Nation under subsection 90(1), the moneys held by Her Majesty for the First Nation’s use and benefit shall be paid to the First Nation out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, if the Minister is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the council has made a law respecting the financial administration of the First Nation under paragraph 9(1)(a) and the law has been approved by the First Nations Financial Management Board;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the council has obtained independent legal advice and independent financial advice with respect to the risks of the payment of the moneys to the First Nation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the payment of the moneys to the First Nation has been approved under section 91.

Subsection.Text: Following a payment of moneys under subsection (1), all moneys subsequently collected or received by Her Majesty for the use and benefit of the First Nation shall be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the First Nation.

Subsection (2) shall cease to apply if the law referred to in paragraph (1)(a) is repealed, and at the time of its repeal, it is not concurrently replaced by a law respecting the financial administration of the First Nation made under paragraph 9(1)(a) that has been approved by the First Nations Financial Management Board.

Section.Text: Following the payment of moneys under section 92, Her Majesty is not liable for the management of those moneys.
This Act does not affect the liability of Her Majesty or a First Nation for any act or omission in respect of moneys occurring before a payment referred to in section 93.
Sections 61 to 69 of the Indian Act do not apply in respect of moneys paid to a First Nation under section 92 of this Act.
Subsection 125(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: At the request of the auditor or examiner of an institution, the present or former commissioners, directors, officers, employees or agents or mandataries of the institution shall provide any information and explanations, and give access to any records, documents, books, accounts and vouchers of the institution that are under their control, that the auditor or examiner considers necessary to prepare a report required under this Act.

Section.Text: Section 136 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Limit of liability — commissioner, director, employee, etc.
Section.Text: No civil proceedings lie against a commissioner or employee of the First Nations Tax Commission, a director or employee of the First Nations Financial Management Board or a person acting on behalf of the Commission or Board for anything done, or omitted to be done, in the exercise or purported exercise in good faith of any power, or in the performance or purported performance in good faith of any duty, of that person in accordance with this Act or regulations made under this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Limit of liability — co-management or third-party management
Section.Text: Despite anything in federal or provincial law, if the First Nations Financial Management Board has required a First Nation to enter into a co-management arrangement in respect of the First Nation’s local revenues in accordance with section 52 or has assumed management of a First Nation’s local revenues in accordance with section 53, neither the Board nor any director or employee of the Board or person acting on behalf of the Board is by reason of that fact liable for any liability of the First Nation.
No director or employee of the First Nations Financial Management Board or person acting on behalf of the Board is personally liable for costs

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: awarded in any civil proceedings brought against any of them in relation to anything done, or omitted to be done, in the exercise or purported exercise of any power, or in the performance or purported performance of any duty, of that person or the Board in accordance with this Act or the regulations made under this Act, unless a court otherwise directs; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: awarded against the Board in any civil proceedings.

Section.Text: Section 141 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 141(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If the regulations made under subsection (1) include a schedule listing the Aboriginal groups that are subject to those regulations, the Minister may, by order, at the request of the governing body of an Aboriginal group referred to in that subsection, amend the schedule in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: add or change the name of the Aboriginal group; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: delete the name of the Aboriginal group, as long as there are no amounts owing by the Aboriginal group to the First Nations Finance Authority that remain unpaid.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 141:

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations — organizations referred to in paragraph 50.1(1)(e)
Section.Text: For the purpose of enabling an organization referred to in paragraph 50.1(1)(e) to benefit from the provisions of this Act, other than Parts 1, 2 and 5, or obtain the services of the First Nations Financial Management Board or the First Nations Finance Authority, the Governor in Council may make any regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adapting any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: restricting the application of any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act.

Section.Text: For the purpose of enabling a First Nation to benefit from the provisions of this Act, or obtain the services of any body established under this Act, in respect of reserve lands that have been set apart for the use and benefit of that First Nation and one or more other First Nations, the Governor in Council may make any regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adapting any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: restricting the application of any provision of this Act or of any regulation made under this Act.

Section.Text: The English version of the Act is amended by replacing “aboriginal” with “Aboriginal” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the preamble;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 3; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 141.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of “first nation”, “first nation’s” and “first nations”
Section.Text: The English version of the Act is amended by replacing “first nation”, “first nation’s” and “first nations” with “First Nation”, “First Nation’s” and “First Nations”, respectively, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the long title;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the preamble;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the definitions borrowing member, first nation, local revenues and third-party management in subsection 2(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 4;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 5(1)(e)(vi) and paragraph 5(1)(g);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 6(1), (2) and (4);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 8(2) and (5);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 9(1) and (5);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 10(2);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 13(1);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: section 13.1;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 14(1) and (1.1) and paragraph 14(2)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: section 15;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(5);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: sections 29 and 30;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 32(1)(a) and (b);

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 33(1)(a) and (b) and subsections 33(2) and (3);

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 35(1)(e);

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 36(1) and paragraph 36(3)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 41(4);

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: sections 49 and 50;

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: sections 51 and 52;

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 53(1), the portion of subsection 53(2) before paragraph (b) and subsections 53(3) to (6) and (8);

Paragraph.Label: (x)
Paragraph.Text: section 54;

Paragraph.Label: (y)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 55(1)(c) and (2)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (z)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 56(a) and (b);

Paragraph.Label: (z.1)
Paragraph.Text: the definitions investing member, property tax revenues and representative in section 57;

Paragraph.Label: (z.2)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 74(e);

Paragraph.Label: (z.3)
Paragraph.Text: sections 76 and 77;

Paragraph.Label: (z.4)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 78(1);

Paragraph.Label: (z.5)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 86(4);

Paragraph.Label: (z.6)
Paragraph.Text: sections 137 and 138; and

Paragraph.Label: (z.7)
Paragraph.Text: sections 145 and 145.1.

Section.Text: Subsection 6.2(1) of the Export and Import Permits Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If any goods have been included on the Import Control List under subsection 5(6) or for the purpose of implementing an intergovernmental arrangement or commitment, the Minister may determine import access quantities, or the basis for calculating them, for the purposes of subsection (2) and section 8.3 of this Act and for the purposes of the Customs Tariff.

Section.Text: The Pay Equity Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish a proactive pay equity regime within the federal public and private sectors

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Pay Equity Act.

The purpose of this Act is to achieve pay equity through proactive means by redressing the systemic gender-based discrimination in the compensation practices and systems of employers that is experienced by employees who occupy positions in predominantly female job classes so that they receive equal compensation for work of equal value, while taking into account the diverse needs of employers, and then to maintain pay equity through proactive means.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: bank rate means the rate of interest established periodically by the Bank of Canada as the minimum rate at which the Bank of Canada makes short term advances to members of the Canadian Payments Association. (taux d’escompte)

bargaining agent has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Public Sector Labour Relations Act or the same meaning as in subsection 3(1) of the Canada Labour Code, as the case may be. (agent négociateur)

bargaining unit has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Public Sector Labour Relations Act or the same meaning as in subsection 3(1) of the Canada Labour Code, as the case may be. (unité de négociation)
compensation means any form of remuneration payable for work performed by an employee and includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: salaries, commissions, vacation pay, severance pay and bonuses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: payments in kind;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: employer contributions to pension funds or plans, long-term disability plans and all forms of health insurance plans; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other advantage received directly or indirectly from the employer. (rémunération)

Definition.Text: core public administration has the same meaning as in subsection 11(1) of the Financial Administration Act. (administration publique centrale)
dependent contractor has the same meaning as in subsection 3(1) of the Canada Labour Code. (entrepreneur dépendant)
employee means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed in the public service, other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person appointed by the Governor in Council under an Act of Parliament to a statutory position described in that Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person locally engaged outside Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person employed under a program designated by the employer as a student employment program;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an officer or non-commissioned member of the Canadian Forces;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by an employer referred to in paragraph (2)(e) on or in connection with the operation of any federal work, undertaking or business, as defined in section 2 of the Canada Labour Code, including a private constable, as defined in subsection 3(1) of that Act, other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person employed under a program designated by the employer as a student employment program, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a student employed by the employer solely during the student’s vacation periods;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by an employer referred to in paragraph (2)(e.1), other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person employed under a program designated by the employer as a student employment program, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a student employed by the employer solely during the student’s vacation periods;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an employer referred to in paragraph (2)(f), a dependent contractor;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by the government of Yukon;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by the government of the Northwest Territories; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by the government of Nunavut. (employé)

Definition.Text: employee organization has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Public Sector Labour Relations Act. (organisation syndicale)

group of job classes means a series of job classes that bear a relationship to each other because of the nature of the work required to be performed within each job class in the series and that are organized in successive levels. (groupe de catégories d’emplois)
Minister means the member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada designated under section 5. (ministre)

non-unionized employee means an employee who is not a member of a bargaining unit, other than an employee who occupies a position declared to be a managerial or confidential position as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Public Sector Labour Relations Act or an employee who performs management functions or is employed in a confidential capacity in matters relating to industrial relations. (non syndiqué)
Pay Equity Commissioner means the Pay Equity Commissioner appointed under subsection 26(1) of the Canadian Human Rights Act. (Commissaire à l’équité salariale)
payroll, in respect of an employer, means the total of all salaries payable to the employer’s employees. (masse salariale)
public service means the several positions in or under

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the departments named in Schedule I to the Financial Administration Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the other portions of the federal public administration named in Schedule IV to that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the separate agencies named in Schedule V to that Act. (fonction publique)

Definition.Text: trade union has the same meaning as in subsection 3(1) of the Canada Labour Code. (syndicat)
Tribunal means the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal established by section 48.1 of the Canadian Human Rights Act. (Tribunal)
unionized employee means an employee who is a member of a bargaining unit that is represented by a bargaining agent. (syndiqué)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, each of the following is considered to be an employer:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, in respect of the aggregate of the departments and other portions of the federal public administration named in Schedules I and IV, respectively, to the Financial Administration Act, except that the reference to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police in that Schedule IV is deemed to be a reference to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police only in respect of civilian employees appointed or employed in accordance with section 10 of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, in respect of the Canadian Forces;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, in respect of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, in relation to members as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a separate agency, as defined in subsection 11(1) of the Financial Administration Act, Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the separate agency;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: each person who employs employees in connection with the operation of any federal work, undertaking or business, as defined in section 2 of the Canada Labour Code, other than a work, undertaking or business of a local or private nature in Yukon, the Northwest Territories or Nunavut;

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation established to perform any duty or function on behalf of the Government of Canada, other than a corporation named in Schedule IV or V of the Financial Administration Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a dependent contractor, an employer as defined in paragraph (b) of that expression in subsection 3(1) of the Canada Labour Code;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the government of Yukon;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the government of the Northwest Territories; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the government of Nunavut.

Subsection.MarginalNote: References to employees in regards to Her Majesty in right of Canada
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, every reference to an employer’s employees is to be read as a reference to,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, as employer in respect of the aggregate of the departments and other portions of the federal public administration referred to in paragraph (2)(a), only employees employed in those departments and other portions, other than members as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, as employer in respect of the Canadian Forces, only officers and non-commissioned members of the Canadian Forces;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by the Treasury Board, as employer in respect of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, only members as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of Her Majesty in right of Canada as represented by a separate agency, as employer in respect of the separate agency, only employees employed in that separate agency.

Subsection.Text: Two or more employers described in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) that are subject to this Act may form a group and apply to the Pay Equity Commissioner to have the group of employers recognized as a single employer.

If a group of employers is recognized by the Pay Equity Commissioner as a single employer under section 106, every reference in sections 19 to 21, 27, 32, 35, 38 to 41, 43 to 50 and 54, subsection 57(1), sections 64, 75, 78, 79 and 82, subsections 85(1) and 104(2), sections 111, 147, 148 and 151, subsection 157(1) and paragraph 181(1)(c) to an employer is, in respect of the group of employers, to be read as a reference to that group of employers, unless the context otherwise requires.

If a group of employers is recognized by the Pay Equity Commissioner as a single employer under section 106, every reference in sections 118 to 120, subsections 149(2) and 150(3), paragraph 158(1)(b), subsections 158(2) and (3) and section 168 to an employer may, in respect of the group of employers, is to be read as a reference to that group of employers or to any employer in the group, as the context requires.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date on which group becomes subject to Act
Subsection.Text: If the Pay Equity Commissioner recognizes a group of employers as a single employer, the date on which the group of employers is considered to have become subject to this Act for the purpose of subsection 55(1) and paragraphs 61(1)(b) and 89(2)(b) is the date chosen by that Commissioner.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate a member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada as the Minister for the purposes of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Employers subject to Act on coming into force
Section.Text: The following employers become subject to this Act on the date on which this section comes into force:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (d) that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is considered under subparagraph 8(a)(i) to have 10 to 99 employees, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is considered under subparagraph 8(b)(i) to have 100 or more employees; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is considered under subparagraph 9(a)(i) to have 10 to 99 employees, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is considered under subparagraph 9(b)(i) to have 100 or more employees.

Section.MarginalNote: Later date in public sector — 10 to 99 employees

Subsection.Text: An employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (d) that is considered under subparagraph 8(a)(ii) to have 10 to 99 employees becomes subject to this Act on the first day of the fiscal year immediately after the fiscal year in which the average of the number of the employer’s employees was at least 10 but less than 100.

An employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (d) that is considered under subparagraph 8(b)(ii) to have 100 or more employees becomes subject to this Act on the first day of the fiscal year immediately after the fiscal year in which the average of the number of the employer’s employees was 100 or more.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Private sector and territorial governments — 10 to 99 employees
Subsection.Text: An employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) that is considered under subparagraph 9(a)(ii) to have 10 to 99 employees becomes subject to this Act on January 1 of the calendar year immediately after the calendar year in which the average of the number of the employer’s employees was at least 10 but less than 100.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Private sector and territorial governments — 100 or more employees
Subsection.Text: An employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) that is considered under subparagraph 9(b)(ii) to have 100 or more employees becomes subject to this Act on January 1 of the calendar year immediately after the calendar year in which the average of the number of the employer’s employees was more than 100.

Section.MarginalNote: Determination of number of employees — public sector
Section.Text: For the purpose of sections 6 and 7

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (d) is considered to have 10 to 99 employees if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the fiscal year immediately before the fiscal year in which this section comes into force is at least 10 but less than 100, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the fiscal year in which this section comes into force or in any subsequent fiscal year is at least 10 but less than 100; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(a) to (d) is considered to have 100 or more employees if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the fiscal year immediately before the fiscal year in which this section comes into force is 100 or more, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the fiscal year in which this section comes into force or in any subsequent fiscal year is 100 or more.

Section.MarginalNote: Determination of number of employees — private sector and territorial governments
Section.Text: For the purpose of sections 6 and 7

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) is considered to have 10 to 99 employees if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the calendar year immediately before the calendar year in which this section comes into force is at least 10 but less than 100, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the calendar year in which this section comes into force or in any subsequent calendar year is at least 10 but less than 100; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an employer referred to in any of paragraphs 3(2)(e) to (i) is considered to have 100 or more employees if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the calendar year immediately before the calendar year in which this section comes into force is 100 or more, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the average of the number of the employer’s employees in the calendar year in which this section comes into force or in any subsequent calendar year is 100 or more.

Section.Text: The governments of Yukon, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut are exempt from the application of this Act until the date that the Governor in Council may, by order, specify in respect of that government.

Subsection.Text: Indigenous governing bodies that are employers are exempt from the application of this Act until the date that the Governor in Council may, by order, specify.

In this section, Indigenous governing body means a council, government or other entity that is authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group, community or people that holds rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: Every employer must establish a pay equity plan in accordance with this Act in respect of its employees.

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to establish plan — group of employers
Section.Text: Every group of employers must establish a pay equity plan in accordance with this Act in respect of the employees of the employers in the group.

Section.MarginalNote: Notice — employers referred to in subsection 16(1)

Subsection.Text: An employer referred to in subsection 16(1) must post a notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: setting out its obligation

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to establish a pay equity plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to make all reasonable efforts to establish a pay equity committee for that purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: setting out the requirements for the committee’s membership;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: informing its non-unionized employees, if any, of their right to designate the committee members who will represent them; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: informing its unionized employees, if any, that their bargaining agent will select the committee members who will represent the employees who are members of any bargaining unit represented by that bargaining agent.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice — employers referred to in subsection 16(2)
Subsection.Text: An employer referred to in subsection 16(2) must post a notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: setting out its obligation to establish a pay equity plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if it has decided to establish a pay equity committee,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: setting out the requirements for the committee’s membership, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: informing its employees of their right to designate the committee members who will represent them.

Section.MarginalNote: Notice — group of employers referred to in subsection 17(1)

Subsection.Text: Each employer that is in a group of employers referred to in subsection 17(1) must post a notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: indicating that it is in a group of employers;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: setting out the group’s obligation

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to establish a pay equity plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to make all reasonable efforts to establish a pay equity committee for that purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: setting out the requirements for the committee’s membership;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: informing the employer’s non-unionized employees, if any, of their right to designate the committee members who will represent the non-unionized employees of all of the employers in the group of employers; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: informing the employer’s unionized employees, if any, that their bargaining agent will select the committee members who will represent the employees — of all of the employers in the group of employers — who are members of any bargaining unit represented by that bargaining agent.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice — group of employers referred to in subsection 17(2)
Subsection.Text: Each employer that is in a group of employers referred to in subsection 17(2) must post a notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: indicating that it is in a group of employers;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: setting out the group’s obligation to establish a pay equity plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the group has decided to establish a pay equity committee,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: setting out the requirements for the committee’s membership, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: informing the employer’s employees of their right to designate the committee members who will represent the employees of all of the employers in the group of employers.

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to establish pay equity committee

Subsection.Text: The following employers must, in respect of the pay equity plan that the employer is required to establish, make all reasonable efforts to establish a pay equity committee:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an employer that is considered to have 100 or more employees for the purpose of section 6 or 7, as the case may be; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
