The Administrator may, within three years after the day of the occurrence in respect of which the claim was made, investigate and reassess any claim for which a payment was made under subsection 106.3(4).
For the purpose of investigating and reassessing a claim, the Administrator has the powers of a commissioner under Part I of the Inquiries Act.
When investigating and reassessing a claim, the Administrator may consider only whether the claim meets the conditions set out in subsection 106.1(1).
As soon as feasible after completing the investigation and reassessment, the Administrator shall give the claimant notice that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator will take no further action in respect of the claimant’s claim; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is required to pay to the Administrator the overpayment set out in the notice, in the manner and within the period set out in the notice.

Subsection.Text: The following amounts paid under subsection 106.3(4) constitute an overpayment to the claimant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount paid in respect of a claim that the Administrator is satisfied was not for loss, damage, costs or expenses referred to in paragraph 106.1(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that was paid in respect of a claim that the Administrator is satisfied was not the first one that the claimant has filed in respect of a given occurrence for loss, damage, costs or expenses referred to in that paragraph;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that was paid in respect of a claim that the Administrator is satisfied resulted from an act done or omitted to be done by the claimant with intent to cause damage or from the claimant’s negligence; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that was paid in respect of a claim, if the Administrator is satisfied on the evidence that the occurrence that gave rise to the claim was not caused by a ship.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Overpayments — limitation or prescription period exceeded
Subsection.Text: The Administrator may, in his or her discretion, determine that an amount paid under subsection 106.3(4) constitutes an overpayment to the claimant if the Administrator is satisfied that the amount was paid in respect of a claim that was not filed within the period set out in subsection 106.1(4).

A claimant may, within 60 days after receiving a notice under paragraph (1)(b), appeal to the Admiralty Court the requirement to pay the overpayment set out in the notice referred to in that paragraph.

Section.Text: Unless the claimant under subsection 106.1(1) is a minister of the Crown in right of Canada, all amounts and overpayments payable under subsection 106.4(3) or paragraph 106.6(1)(b), respectively, constitute debts due to Her Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in a court of competent jurisdiction from the person who is required to pay them.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting claims filed under subsection 106.1(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fixing an amount for the purposes of paragraph 106.1(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing information for the purposes of subparagraph 106.1(2)(c)(iv) or paragraph 106.1(2)(d); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: fixing an amount for the purposes of subsection 106.1(3).

Section.Text: In sections 106.1 to 106.6, incident and occurrence have the meaning assigned by the definition incident in Article I of the Civil Liability Convention.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 107(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subject to this section, a claimant who suffers or will suffer a loss of income, or in the case of an individual described in paragraph (2)(d), a loss of a source of food or animal skins, resulting from a discharge of oil from a ship that is not recoverable under Part 6 or any other provision of this Part may file a claim with the Administrator for that loss or future loss
Subsection 107(6) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The heading before section 110 and sections 110 and 111 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Request by Minister of Fisheries and Oceans for funds — $10 million

Subsection.Text: If, in the opinion of the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, emergency funds are necessary to respond to a significant incident involving the discharge of oil from a ship, the Minister of Transport may, after consultation with the Administrator, direct the amount of funds requested by the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, or a lesser amount, to be charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund to enable the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans to respond to the significant incident, to a maximum of $10 million per fiscal year.

If the maximum amount has been charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection (1) and, in the opinion of the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, additional emergency funds are necessary to respond to a significant incident in that fiscal year involving the discharge of oil from a ship, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Transport made after consultation with the Administrator, issue an order in council directing the additional funds requested by the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, or a lesser amount, to be charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund to enable the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans to respond to the significant incident, to a maximum of $50 million per fiscal year.
For greater certainty, the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans may not request emergency funds under subsection (1) or (2) for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: measures taken or to be taken under paragraph 180(1)(a) of the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 in respect only of a discharge of oil by a small vessel that is dilapidated or abandoned; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the carrying out by that Minister of only minor monitoring of measures under paragraph 180(1)(b) of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Fisheries and Oceans may use emergency funds only to respond to a significant incident involving the discharge of oil from a ship, including paying third parties. For greater certainty, that Minister may not use such funds for any

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: measures taken or to be taken under paragraph 180(1)(a) of the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 in respect only of a discharge of oil by a small vessel that is dilapidated or abandoned; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: measures referred to in subsection 101(1.1).

Section.MarginalNote: Reimbursement of amount of emergency funds

Subsection.Text: If emergency funds are charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2), the amount of the emergency funds shall be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund, out of appropriations for the Department of Fisheries and Oceans under an appropriation Act, within two years after the day on which the oil pollution damage resulting from the significant incident occurs or within any longer period agreed to by the Administrator and the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans.

Nothing in subsection (1) affects the rights of the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans under sections 51, 71, 77 and 101, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention and Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention with respect to loss and damage suffered, and costs and expenses incurred, in respect of the significant incident.

If the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans files a claim with the Administrator under section 103 for loss and damage suffered, and costs and expenses incurred, in respect of the significant incident for which emergency funds were charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2), then, despite subsection 111(1), it is only any portion of the emergency funds that is unused at the time of the filing of the claim that is to be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund within two years after the day on which the oil pollution damage resulting from the significant incident occurs or within any longer period agreed to by the Administrator and the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offer of compensation less than zero
Subsection.Text: If the offer of compensation made by the Administrator to the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans under paragraph 105(1)(b) is less than zero, then, unless that Minister appeals the adequacy of the offer under subsection 106(2), an amount equal to the amount of the offer shall be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund, out of appropriations for the Department of Fisheries and Oceans under an appropriation Act, within six months after the day on which that Minister receives the offer or within any longer period agreed to by the Administrator and that Minister.

If the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans refuses the offer of compensation made under paragraph 105(1)(b), then, as soon as feasible after that Minister receives the offer, an amount equal to the amount of the emergency funds charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2) shall be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund, out of appropriations for the Department of Fisheries and Oceans under an appropriation Act, less any unused portion of the emergency funds credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection (1).

If emergency funds are charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2), then, for the purposes of sections 105, 106 and this section, offer of compensation means, with respect to the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, the amount determined in accordance with the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount that the Administrator assessed under paragraph 105(1)(a);
is the amount of emergency funds charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2); and
is the amount of any unused portion of emergency funds credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Interest accrues, at a rate determined in accordance with regulations made under paragraph 155.1(6)(a) of the Financial Administration Act, beginning on the day on which the emergency funds were charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2), on any portion of those funds that are required to be credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 111(1) or 111.1(1) or (2) and have not been so credited within the time within which they are required to be so credited under that subsection.

On request by the Minister of Transport, the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans shall provide the Minister of Transport and the Administrator with information about costs and expenses incurred with emergency funds charged to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under subsection 110(1) or (2).
Section 112 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions in this section apply in sections 113, 114.1, 117.1 and 117.3.

Definition.Text: contributing oil has the same meaning as in paragraph 3 of Article 1 of the Fund Convention. (hydrocarbures donnant lieu à contribution)
non-persistent oil means the oils that are referred to in paragraph 1(a)(ii) of Article 19 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention. (hydrocarbures non persistants)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 113(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The levy is 52.38 cents in the year ending on March 31, 2019 in respect of each metric ton of contributing oil or non-persistent oil received or exported, as the case may be, in bulk as cargo.
Subsection 113(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Adjusted levy to be published annually
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall cause the levy referred to in subsection (1) to be published in the Canada Gazette each year as soon as it is adjusted in accordance with this section, and the levy so published is admissible in any proceeding under this Act as conclusive proof of the levy for the year in question.

Section.Text: Subsection 114(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans, may, by order, impose, discontinue or re-impose the levy referred to in section 113, indefinitely or until a time specified in the order.
An order imposing or re-imposing the levy shall set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day by which an amount equal to the amount of the levy is to be paid in the year in which the levy is imposed or re-imposed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of each subsequent year — if any — for which the levy is imposed or re-imposed, a reference date for the purposes of the levy in that year.

Subsection.Text: In any year referred to in paragraph (1.1)(b), the day by which an amount equal to the amount of the levy is to be paid is the 60th day after the reference date, or any other day after the reference date that is specified in the order imposing or re-imposing the levy.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 114:
For the purposes of paragraphs (2)(a) and (b), the amount to be paid under those paragraphs in respect of the levy for a given year is determined by the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the year in which the levy is imposed or re-imposed by order made under subsection 114(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of metric tons of oil is the number that was required to be reported in the most recent information return that, on the day on which the order is made, was required to be filed under section 117.1, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the levy referred to in subsection 113(1), as adjusted under subsection 113(2), is the levy that is in effect on the day on which the order is made; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any subsequent year for which the levy is imposed or re-imposed by the order,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of metric tons of oil is the number that was required to be reported in the most recent information return that, on the reference date set out in the order for that year, was required to be filed under section 117.1, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the levy referred to in subsection 113(1), as adjusted under subsection 113(2), is the levy that is in effect on the reference date set out in the order for that year.

Subsection.Text: If a levy determined in accordance with section 113 is imposed or re-imposed by an order made under subsection 114(1), then the following persons shall pay to the Receiver General an amount equal to the amount of the levy:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every person who is referred to in Article 10 of the Fund Convention; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every person who is a receiver and receives, in a calendar year, more than 20 000 metric tons — or, if any lesser quantity is fixed by regulations made under paragraph 125(b), that quantity — of non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo.

Subsection.Text: In subsection (2), person includes two or more persons who are associated persons.
In subsection (3), associated persons means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: persons who are deemed to be associated persons under section 60, in the case of persons who are referred to in Article 10 of the Fund Convention; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: persons who are deemed to be associated persons under section 74.3, in the case of persons who are receivers.

Subsection.Text: If an amount is charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund under section 93.1, the Minister may, by order, impose an additional levy — whether or not he or she has made an order under subsection 114(1) imposing or re-imposing a levy — that the Minister specifies in the order on every person referred to in paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) and (b), in which case each of those persons shall pay to the Receiver General, in accordance with the order, an amount equal to the amount of the additional levy.

If such an order is made, the Minister shall revoke it as soon as feasible after an amount equal to the amount charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund under section 93.1 has been credited to the Consolidated Revenue Fund out of amounts standing to the credit of the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund and the Minister is satisfied that the terms and conditions in relation to the charging of that amount under that section have been met.
The portion of subsection 114.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of paragraphs (2)(a) to (d), the amount to be paid under those paragraphs in respect of the levy for a given year is determined by the following rules:
Subsection 114.1(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: every person who exports, in a calendar year, more than 150 000 metric tons — or, if any lesser quantity is fixed by regulations made under paragraph 125(b), that quantity — of contributing oil in bulk as cargo; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: every person who exports, in a calendar year, more than 20 000 metric tons — or, if any lesser quantity is fixed by regulations made under paragraph 125(b), that quantity — of non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114.1(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: persons each of whom is an affiliate of the other as defined in section 2 of the Canada Business Corporations Act, in the case of persons who export.

Subsection.Text: Section 114.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purposes of paragraph (2)(d), if the person who exports the oil acts as an agent or mandatary for another person who is in Canada and the agent or mandatary discloses the principal or mandator to the Administrator, then the principal or mandator is deemed to be the exporter.

No amount is payable by a person referred to in paragraph (2)(b) in respect of a shipment of non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo in respect of which an amount will be payable by a person referred to in paragraph (2)(d).
Subsection 114.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an amount is charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund under section 93.1, the Minister may, by order, impose an additional levy — whether or not he or she has made an order under subsection 114(1) imposing or re-imposing a levy — that the Minister specifies in the order on every person referred to in paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) to (d), in which case each of those persons shall pay to the Receiver General, in accordance with the order, an amount equal to the amount of the additional levy.

Section.Text: Section 115 of the Act is replaced by the following:

All amounts payable under sections 114.1 and 114.2 are debts due to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be recovered in any court of competent jurisdiction from the person who is required to pay them.
Subsections 117(1.1) to (7) of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 117:
The following persons shall file with the Minister or the Administrator, in accordance with the regulations, information returns in respect of the oil in question:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every person who is referred to in Article 10 of the Fund Convention; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every person who is a receiver and receives non-persistent oil in a calendar year in a quantity that is greater than that prescribed by regulations made under paragraph (4)(b).

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), person includes two or more persons who are associated persons.
In subsection (2), associated persons means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: persons who are deemed to be associated persons under section 60, in the case of persons who are referred to in Article 10 of the Fund Convention; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: persons who are deemed to be associated persons under section 74.3, in the case of persons who are receivers.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of subsection (1), information returns; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of paragraph (1)(b), a quantity of non-persistent oil.

Subsection.Text: The Administrator shall,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in accordance with Article 15 of the Fund Convention, communicate the information referred to in that Article to the Minister and the Director of the International Fund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in accordance with Article 13 of the Supplementary Fund Protocol, communicate the information referred to in that Article to the Minister and the Director of the Supplementary Fund.

Subsection.Text: The Administrator shall communicate to the Minister the information referred to in subsection 74.4(4) that relates to oils described in paragraph 5(a)(i) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention and that is necessary to enable the Minister to discharge his or her obligation under that subsection.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Communication to Minister and Director of HNS Fund
Subsection.Text: The Administrator shall, in accordance with Article 21 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, communicate to the Minister and the Director of the HNS Fund the information referred to in that Article that relates to oils described in paragraph 5(a)(i) of Article 1 of that Convention.
The Administrator is liable for any financial loss to the International Fund or the Supplementary Fund, as the case may be, as a result of a failure to communicate the information.
The Administrator may,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3), at any reasonable time, enter a place in which he or she has reasonable grounds to believe there are any records, books of account, accounts, vouchers or other documents relating to information referred to in Article 15 of the Fund Convention, Article 13 of the Supplementary Fund Protocol or Article 21 or 45 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3), examine anything at the place and copy or take away for further examination or copying any record, book of account, account, voucher or other document that he or she has reasonable grounds to believe contains any such information; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3), require the owner, occupier or person in charge of the place to give the Administrator all reasonable assistance in connection with the examination and to answer all proper questions relating to the examination and, for that purpose, require the owner, occupier or person in charge to attend at the place with the Administrator.

Subsection.Text: No person shall knowingly obstruct or hinder the Administrator in the exercise of any powers under subsection (1) or knowingly make a false or misleading statement, either orally or in writing, to the Administrator while he or she is exercising those powers.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Warrant required to enter dwelling place
Subsection.Text: A dwelling place may not be entered under subsection (1) unless it is entered with the occupant’s consent or under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (4).

On ex parte application, a justice, as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code, may issue a warrant authorizing the Administrator to enter a dwelling place, subject to any conditions that may be specified in the warrant, if the justice is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the dwelling place is a place referred to in paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling place is necessary for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling place has been refused or there are reasonable grounds to believe that it will be refused.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 117.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following persons shall file with the Administrator, in accordance with the regulations, information returns in respect of the oil in question:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every person who exports, in a calendar year, contributing oil in bulk as cargo in a quantity that is greater than that prescribed by regulations made under paragraph (4)(c); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every person who exports, in a calendar year, non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo in a quantity that is greater than that prescribed by regulations made under paragraph (4)(d).

Subsection.Text: In subsections (1) and (1.1), person includes two or more persons who are associated persons.
Subsection 117.1(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: persons each of whom is an affiliate of the other as defined in section 2 of the Canada Business Corporations Act, in the case of persons who export.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 117.1(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of subsections (1) and (1.1), information returns;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 117.1(4) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of paragraph (1.1)(a), a quantity of contributing oil; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of paragraph (1.1)(b), a quantity of non-persistent oil.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 117.3(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1.1), at any reasonable time, enter a place in which he or she has reasonable grounds to believe there are any records, books of account, accounts, vouchers or other documents relating to the export of contributing oil or non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3), examine anything at the place and copy or take away for further examination or copying any record, book of account, account, voucher or other document that he or she has reasonable grounds to believe contains any such information; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3), require the owner, occupier or person in charge of the place to give the Administrator all reasonable assistance in connection with the examination and to answer all proper questions relating to the examination and, for that purpose, require the owner, occupier or person in charge to attend at the place with the Administrator.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 117.3(4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the dwelling place is a place referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (a.1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling place is necessary for the purposes of subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) or 117.2(1), (2) or (3); and

Subsection.Text: Subsections 118(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Every person who is required to file information returns under subsection 117.1(1) shall keep at their place of business in Canada, or at any other place in Canada that is designated by the Minister, in relation to the oil that they are required to file information returns in respect of, records that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: set out the type and quantity of the oil received or exported, as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contain any other information or documents that the Minister directs them to provide under section 118.1.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Records and books of account — payers of levy
Subsection.Text: Every person referred to in subsection (1) who is required to pay an amount under section 114.1 or 114.2 shall keep at their place of business in Canada, or at any other place in Canada that is designated by the Minister, records and books of account that set out, in addition to the information referred to in subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amounts that are payable by that person under section 114.1 or 114.2 in respect of the types and quantities of oil referred to in subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proof of payment for each payment made with respect to an amount referred to in paragraph (a), including the date of each payment made.

Subsection.Text: Every person who is required by this section to keep records or books of account shall, unless otherwise authorized by the Minister, retain those records or books of account, and every account or voucher necessary to verify the information contained in them, until the expiry of six years after the end of the year to which the records or books of account relate.
The portion of subsection 118(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Every person who is required to file information returns under subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) shall keep at their place of business in Canada, or at any other place in Canada that is designated by the Minister, in relation to the oil that they are required to file information returns in respect of, records that
Subsection 118(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who is required by this section to keep records or books of account shall, at all reasonable times, make the records or books of account, and every account or voucher necessary to verify the information contained in them, available to any person designated in writing by the Minister and give that person every facility necessary to examine them.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 118:

If the Minister directs that a person referred to in subsection 118(1) provide, in the specified form and within the specified period, information or documents that the Minister considers necessary for the purposes of ensuring compliance with this Part, the person shall provide that information in that form and within that period.
The portion of subsection 119(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any person so designated in writing by the Minister may, at any reasonable time, enter any place in which the person has reasonable grounds to believe that there are any records, books of account, accounts, vouchers or other documents referred to in section 118 and
Paragraphs 120(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: records and books of account to be kept in relation to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: control and information systems and management practices, in respect of financial and management matters, to be maintained in relation to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 120(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Administrator shall cause the books, records, systems and practices to be kept or maintained, as the case may be, in a manner that provides reasonable assurance that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator’s and Deputy Administrator’s powers, duties and functions under this Part are exercised and performed effectively and in accordance with this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the assets used by them are safeguarded and controlled; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the financial, human and physical resources used by them are managed economically and efficiently.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 121(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Administrator shall as soon as feasible, but in any case within three months after the end of each fiscal year, submit an annual report, in any form that the Minister directs, on the Administrator’s activities in that year to the Minister, who shall cause a copy of the report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after he or she receives it.
Paragraphs 121(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the amounts charged and credited to the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund during the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the costs and expenses incurred during the fiscal year by the Administrator and the Deputy Administrator in exercising their powers and performing their duties and functions under this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the fees for services rendered by the Administrator and the Deputy Administrator during the fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor’s report with respect to the statements referred to in paragraphs (a) to (a.2); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 121(2)(c) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: les frais de préparation du rapport du vérificateur.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 122(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Administrator shall cause a special examination to be carried out in respect of the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund to determine if the Fund’s systems and practices referred to in paragraph 120(1)(b) were, in the period under examination, maintained in a manner that provided reasonable assurance that they met the requirements of paragraphs 120(2)(b) and (c).
Subsection 122(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Les examens spéciaux par l’administrateur sont au moins quinquennaux; des examens spéciaux complémentaires ont lieu à la demande du gouverneur en conseil ou du ministre.
Subsections 122(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Administrator shall appoint a person to act as examiner for the purpose of conducting a special examination. However, if a special examination is required by the Governor in Council or the Minister, the Governor in Council or the Minister, as the case may be, shall make the appointment.
The examiner shall not accept or hold any office or employment, or carry on any activity, that is inconsistent with their duties and functions under this section and section 123.

Section.Text: Subsection 123(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On the conclusion of the special examination, an examiner shall provide a written report of their findings to the Minister and the Administrator.
Subsection 124(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the examiner considers it necessary to enable them to prepare a report as required by this Part, they may direct that present or former Administrators, Deputy Administrators, employees or agents or mandataries of the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund provide the examiner with the following to the extent they are reasonably able to do so:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information and explanations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: access to any records, books of account, accounts, vouchers and other documents related to the Fund.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 124(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
On receiving the examiner’s direction, the Administrator shall
Paragraph 124(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: obtain from any former Administrator or the present or any former Deputy Administrator, employees or agents or mandataries of that Fund any information and explanations that the examiner considers necessary to enable the examiner to prepare any report that is required by this Part and that the former Administrator or the present or former Deputy Administrator, employees or agents or mandataries are reasonably able to provide and provide the examiner with the information and explanations so obtained.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 125(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the payment of the levy under section 114.1 and of the additional levy under section 114.2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing, for the purposes of paragraph 114.1(2)(b), a quantity of oil that is less than the quantity set out in that paragraph;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: exempting classes of persons from the application of paragraph 114.1(2)(a) or (b);

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing classes of persons for the purposes of regulations made under paragraph (c);

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: amending the definition receiver in subsection 91(1); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 125(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing, for the purposes of any of paragraphs 114.1(2)(b) to (d), a quantity of oil that is less than the quantity set out in that paragraph;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: exempting classes of persons from the application of any of paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) to (d);

Subsection.Text: Section 125 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c.2) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (c.3)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing classes of persons who export, in a calendar year, more than 150 000 metric tons of contributing oil that is shipped in bulk as cargo after being exported and deeming those persons to have exported the contributing oil in bulk as cargo in that year;

Paragraph.Label: (c.4)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing classes of persons who export, in a calendar year, more than 20 000 metric tons of non-persistent oil that is shipped in bulk as cargo after being exported and deeming those persons to have exported the non-persistent oil in bulk as cargo in that year;

Paragraph.Label: (c.5)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing, for the purposes of paragraph (c.3) or (c.4), a quantity of oil that is less than the quantity set out in that paragraph; and

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 130:

Heading.TitleText: Assurances of Compliance and Notices of Violation

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes any of the following provisions commits a violation and is liable to a penalty:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 74.4(2) and (3), paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) and (b), subsections 114.2(1), 117.1(1), 117.3(2) and 118(1) and (1.1), section 118.1 and subsection 129(7); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a provision of this Act or of the regulations the contravention of which is designated as a violation by a regulation made under paragraph 130.19(a).

Subsection.Text: The maximum amount payable as the penalty for each violation set out in subsection (1) is, in the case of an individual, $50,000 and, in the case of any other person, $250,000.
If a contravention of a provision may be proceeded with as a violation or as an offence, proceeding with it in one manner precludes proceeding with it in the other.
For greater certainty, a violation is not an offence and, accordingly, section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply in respect of a violation.

A person shall not be found to be liable for a violation under this Act, other than in relation to a contravention of subsection 117.3(2) or 118(1) or (1.1), if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent its commission.

Section.MarginalNote: Assurance of compliance or notice of violation

Subsection.Text: If the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has committed a violation, the Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enter into an assurance of compliance with the person that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: identifies the violation and provides that the person will comply with the provision to which the violation relates within the period, and be subject to the terms and conditions, specified in the assurance,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: sets out the amount and form of any security that, pending compliance with the assurance, is to be deposited with the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: sets out the penalty that the person would have been liable to pay for the violation if the assurance had not been entered into; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issue, and cause to be served on the person, a notice of violation that names them, identifies the violation and sets out

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the penalty that the person is liable to pay for the violation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period, being 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, within which the penalty is to be paid or a review is to be requested, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: particulars of the manner in which, and the address at which, the penalty is to be paid or a review is to be requested.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may extend the period specified under subparagraph (1)(a)(i) if the Minister is satisfied that the person is unable to comply with the assurance of compliance for reasons beyond the person’s control.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Short-form descriptions in notices of violation
Subsection.Text: The Minister may establish, in respect of each violation, a short-form description to be used in notices of violation.

A person who enters into an assurance of compliance under paragraph 130.02(1)(a) is, unless a review is requested under subsection (2), deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into.

A person who enters into an assurance of compliance may, within 48 hours after the assurance is signed, unless a notice of default is served within that period under subsection 130.05(1), file a request with the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada for a review of the facts of the violation, in which case the assurance is deemed to be a notice of violation and the person is deemed to have filed a written request for a review of the facts of the violation and the amount of the penalty under paragraph 130.09(1)(b).

Section.MarginalNote: When assurance of compliance complied with
Section.Text: If the Minister is satisfied that a person who has entered into an assurance of compliance under paragraph 130.02(1)(a) has complied with it, he or she shall cause a notice to that effect to be served on the person and, on the service of the notice,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no further proceedings may be taken against the person with respect to the violation in respect of which the assurance was entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any security deposited under subparagraph 130.02(1)(a)(ii) shall be returned to the person.

Section.MarginalNote: When assurance of compliance not complied with

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is of the opinion that a person who has entered into an assurance of compliance under paragraph 130.02(1)(a) has not complied with it, he or she may cause a notice of default to be served on the person to the effect that, unless a member of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada determines under section 130.07 that the assurance has been complied with, or an appeal panel of that Tribunal decides under section 130.1 that the assurance has been complied with,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is liable to pay double the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the security deposited under subparagraph 130.02(1)(a)(ii) is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The notice shall include the address at which, and the date — being 30 days after the day on which the notice is served — on or before which, a request for a review may be filed and the particulars concerning the procedure for requesting a review.
Once served with the notice of default, the person may not deduct from the amount set out in the notice any amount that the person spent under the assurance of compliance.
A notice referred to in any of sections 130.02, 130.04, 130.05 and 130.18 is to be served

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by leaving a copy of it with the individual at any place or with someone who appears to be an adult member of the same household at the individual’s last known address or usual place of residence or, in the case of an individual under the age of 18, with a parent or other person having custody of them or exercising parental authority over them, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by sending a copy of it by registered mail, courier or electronic means to the individual’s last known address or usual place of residence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person other than an individual,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by leaving a copy of it with their representative or with an officer or other individual who appears to control or manage the person’s or representative’s head office or place of business, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by sending a copy of it by registered mail, courier or electronic means to their representative, to an individual referred to in subparagraph (i) or to the person’s or representative’s head office or place of business.

Subsection.Text: Service may be proved by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an acknowledgement of service signed by or on behalf of the person served, specifying the date and location of service;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a certificate of service, signed by the person who effected the service, indicating the name of the person served and the means by which and day on which service was effected; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a record of electronic transmission setting out the date and time of transmission.

Subsection.Text: In the absence of an acknowledgement of service or a certificate of service, service is considered effective,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of service by registered mail or courier, on the 10th day after the day on which the notice is sent, as indicated on the receipt issued by the postal or courier service; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of service by electronic means, on the day on which it is sent, as indicated on the record of transmission.

Subsection.Text: A person served with a notice under subsection 130.05(1) may, no later than the date specified in the notice or within any further time that the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada on application allows, file a written request for a review of the Minister’s decision made under that subsection with the Tribunal.

On receipt of the request, the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada shall specify a time and place for the review and notify the Minister and the person who filed the request of that time and place in writing.

The member of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada who is assigned to conduct the review shall provide the Minister and the person with an opportunity that is consistent with procedural fairness and natural justice to present evidence and make representations at the review.

The burden is on the Minister to establish that the person did not comply with the assurance of compliance referred to in the notice. The person is not required, and shall not be compelled, to give any evidence or testimony in the matter.
Despite subsection 130.01(5), a person does not have a defence by reason that the person exercised due diligence to comply with the assurance of compliance.

At the conclusion of the review, the member who conducts the review shall confirm the Minister’s decision or determine that the person has complied with the assurance of compliance and shall without delay inform the parties of his or her decision.

Section.Text: Any security deposited under subparagraph 130.02(1)(a)(ii) shall be returned to the person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice is served under subsection 130.05(1) and the person pays double the amount of the penalty set out in the assurance of compliance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada determines under subsection 130.07(6), or an appeal panel of that Tribunal decides under subsection 130.1(3), that the assurance has been complied with.

Subsection.Text: A person served with a notice of violation under paragraph 130.02(1)(b) shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: pay the amount of the penalty; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served or any further time that the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada on application allows, file with the Tribunal a written request for a review of the facts of the violation or the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Amount paid or no review requested
Subsection.Text: If a review is not requested within the period referred to in paragraph (1)(b), or the amount of the penalty is paid, the person is considered to have committed the violation in respect of which the notice was served and any proceedings in respect of the violation are ended.

On receipt of a request filed under paragraph (1)(b), the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada shall specify a time and place for the review and notify the Minister and the person who filed the request of that time and place in writing.

The member of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada who is assigned to conduct the review shall provide the Minister and the person with an opportunity that is consistent with procedural fairness and natural justice to present evidence and make representations at the review.

In every case when the facts of a violation are reviewed, the burden is on the Minister to establish that the person committed the violation referred to in the notice. The person is not required, and shall not be compelled, to give any evidence or testimony in the matter.

At the conclusion of the review, the member who conducts the review shall, without delay and in writing, inform the Minister and the person who is alleged to have committed a violation of the member’s determination.

If the member determines that the person has not committed a violation, then, subject to section 130.1, no further proceedings under this Act may be taken against the person in respect of the alleged violation.

If the member determines that the person has committed a violation, then the member shall inform the Minister and the person, subject to subsection 130.01(2) and regulations made under paragraph 130.19(b), of the amount determined by the member to be payable to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada by or on behalf of the person and the period within which it is to be paid.

The Minister or the person who requested the review may, within 30 days after the day on which a determination is made under subsection 130.07(6) or 130.09(6), appeal the determination to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada.
A party that does not appear at the review hearing is not entitled to appeal the determination, unless they establish that there was sufficient reason to justify their absence.
At the conclusion of the review, the appeal panel of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada that is assigned to hear the appeal shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under subsection 130.07(6), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and substitute its own decision; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under subsection 130.09(6), dismiss the appeal or allow the appeal and, subject to subsection 130.01(2) and regulations made under paragraph 130.19(b), substitute its own decision.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The appeal panel shall inform the parties of its decision without delay and specify the period within which any amount determined by the appeal panel to be payable to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada is to be paid.

Subsection.Text: The following amounts constitute debts due to Her Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in the Admiralty Court or any other court of competent jurisdiction:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of a penalty set out in a notice of violation referred to in paragraph 130.02(1)(b), from the expiry of the period specified in the notice for the payment of that amount, unless a review is requested under paragraph 130.09(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount referred to in paragraph 130.05(1)(a) that a person is liable to pay, from the time the notice of default referred to in subsection 130.05(1) is served, unless a review is requested under subsection 130.07(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of a penalty determined by a member under section 130.09 or an appeal panel under section 130.1, from the expiry of the period specified in the decision for the payment of that amount; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of any costs and expenses referred to in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: Proceedings to recover a debt referred to in subsection (1) may be commenced no later than five years after the day on which the debt becomes payable.
A person who is liable to pay an amount referred to in any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) is also liable for the amount of any costs and expenses incurred in attempting to recover that amount.

All or part of a debt referred to in subsection 130.11(1) in respect of which there is a default of payment may be certified by the Minister or the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada, as the case may be.

On production to the Admiralty Court, a certificate made under subsection (1) is to be registered in that Court and, when registered, has the same force and effect, and proceedings may be taken in connection with it, as if it were a judgment obtained in that Court for a debt of the amount specified in it and all costs and expenses attendant on its registration.

Section.MarginalNote: Party to violation committed by corporation
Section.Text: If a corporation commits a violation under this Act, any director, officer or agent or mandatary of the corporation that directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the violation is a party to and liable for the violation, whether or not the corporation has been identified or proceeded against under sections 130.02 to 130.12.

Section.MarginalNote: Violation by employee or agent or mandatary
Section.Text: A person is liable for a violation that is committed by the person’s employee or agent or mandatary, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary who actually committed the violation is identified or proceeded against under this Act.
No notice of violation may be issued more than two years after the day on which the Minister becomes aware of the violation.

A document that purports to have been issued by the Minister and that certifies the day on which he or she became aware of the violation is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it and, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, is evidence that the Minister became aware of the violation on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: Notices of violation and of default
Section.Text: The Minister may keep a public record of notices of violation and notices of default, including, with respect to each violation or default, the nature of the violation or default, the name of the person who committed it and the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.Text: Unless the Minister is of the opinion that it is not in the public interest to do so, the Minister shall remove from the public record of notices of violation and notices of default, on the fifth anniversary of the day on which a person has paid every penalty that they are liable to pay under this Act, the notice of violation or notice of default along with all the other information kept in the public record with respect to the violation or default.
If the Minister is of the opinion that the removal is not in the public interest, he or she shall give notice of that fact to the person in writing and provide the grounds for that opinion.

The notice shall include the address at which, and the date — being 30 days after the day on which the notice is served — on or before which, a request for a review may be filed and the particulars concerning the procedure for requesting a review.

The person may, no later than the date specified in the notice or within any further time that the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada on application may allow, file with the Tribunal a written request for a review of the Minister’s decision made under subsection (2).

On receipt of the request, the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada shall specify a time and place for the review and notify the Minister and the person who filed the request of that time and place in writing.

The member of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada who is assigned to conduct the review shall provide the Minister and the person with an opportunity that is consistent with procedural fairness and natural justice to present evidence and make representations at the review.
The member who conducts the review shall confirm the Minister’s decision or refer the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration.
The person who requested the review may, within 30 days after the day on which a determination is made under subsection (7), appeal the determination to the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada.
If the person does not appear at the review hearing, the person is not entitled to appeal the determination, unless they establish that there was sufficient reason to justify their absence.
The appeal panel of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada that is assigned to hear the appeal shall dismiss it or refer the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration.

Section.Text: For the purposes of sections 130.01 to 130.18, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: designating as a violation the contravention of any specified provision of this Act or of the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing a penalty or a range of penalties in respect of a violation up to the maximum amount set out in subsection 130.01(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a range of penalties is established by regulations made under paragraph (b), respecting the method of determining the amount payable as the penalty for the violation, including the criteria to be taken into account.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 130.01(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 74.4(2) and (3), any of paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) to (d), subsections 114.2(1), 117.1(1) and (1.1), 117.3(2) and 118(1) and (1.1), section 118.1 and subsection 129(7);

Subsection.Text: Sections 131 and 132 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Every person or ship that contravenes subsection 55(1) or (2), 73(1) or (2), 128(2) or 129(6) or (7) or regulations made under section 39 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes paragraph 114.1(2)(a) or (b) or subsection 114.2(1) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: False or misleading information return — receiver
Subsection.Text: Every receiver who files an information return under subsection 74.4(2) that contains a material statement that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Falsifying or destroying books of account
Subsection.Text: Every person who knowingly destroys, mutilates or falsifies, or who knowingly makes any false or misleading entry or statement in, any record or book of account required to be kept under subsection 118(1) or (1.1) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Every person who knowingly omits a material entry or statement from any record or book of account required to be kept under subsection 118(1) or (1.1) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.
Every person who contravenes subsection 74.4(7) or 117.3(2), section 118 or subsection 119(9) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to file information return — receiver
Subsection.Text: Every receiver who contravenes subsection 74.4(2) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Every person who files a claim under subsection 106.1(1) that includes information that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to file information return — person
Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection 117.1(1) or section 118.1 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: False or misleading information return — person
Subsection.Text: Every person who files an information return under subsection 117.1(1) that contains a material statement that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Every person who provides information or a document under section 118.1 that contains a material statement that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Section.Text: A person shall not be found guilty of an offence under section 131 or any of subsections 132(1), (4.1) and (6) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 132(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person who contravenes any of paragraphs 114.1(2)(a) to (d) or subsection 114.2(1) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.
Subsections 132(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to file information return — person
Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) or section 118.1 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Subsection.MarginalNote: False or misleading information return — person
Subsection.Text: Every person who files an information return under subsection 117.1(1) or (1.1) that contains a material statement that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.
In this section, other Act means the Safeguarding Canada’s Seas and Skies Act.
On the first day on which both section 33 of the other Act and section 713 of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 74.24 of the Marine Liability Act is renumbered as subsection 74.24(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability — grave and imminent threat of pollution damage
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), with respect to the costs and expenses incurred by the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans or any other person, including in respect of preventive measures referred to in paragraph (1)(b), the owner of a ship is liable only for the costs and expenses related to any occurrence, or series of occurrences having the same origin, that causes pollution damage or creates a grave and imminent threat of causing such damage.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 111(2) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Nothing in subsection (1) affects the rights of the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans under sections 51, 71, 74.24, 77 and 101, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention and Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention with respect to loss and damage suffered, and costs and expenses incurred, in respect of the significant incident.
On the first day on which both subsection 41(1) of the other Act and section 721 of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 101(1) of the Marine Liability Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Liability of Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund

Subsection.Text: Subject to the other provisions of this Part, the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund is liable in relation to oil for any kind of loss, damage, costs or expenses — including liability for economic loss caused by oil pollution suffered by persons whose property has not been polluted, but not including liability for loss in relation to damage as defined in paragraph 6(a) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention — for the matters referred to in sections 51, 71, 74.24 and 77, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention and Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, if

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 101(1.1) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Additional liability of Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund
Subsection.Text: Subject to the other provisions of this Part, the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund is liable for the costs and expenses incurred by the Minister of Fisheries and Oceans or any other person in respect of measures taken under subsection 180(1) of the Canada Shipping Act, 2001 with respect to oil, or for loss or damage caused by those measures, if, by reason of the fact that the occurrence or series of occurrences for which those costs and expenses were incurred did not create a grave and imminent threat of causing oil pollution damage, neither the owner of a ship, the International Fund, the Supplementary Fund nor the HNS Fund is liable for those costs and expenses.
If subsection 42(1) of the other Act comes into force before subsection 722(1) of this Act, then the French version of that subsection 722(1) is replaced by the following:
L’alinéa 102(1)a) de la même loi est remplacé par ce qui suit :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: d’une part, même avant d’avoir reçu la demande visée à l’article 103 ou 106.1, intenter une action réelle contre le navire qui fait l’objet de la demande ou à l’égard du produit de la vente de celui-ci déposé au tribunal;

Subsection.Text: If subsection 722(1) of this Act comes into force before subsection 42(1) of the other Act, then paragraph 102(1)(a) of the Marine Liability Act, as enacted by that subsection 42(1), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator may, either before or after receiving a claim under section 103 or 106.1, commence an action in rem against the ship that is the subject of the claim, or against any proceeds of sale of the ship that have been paid into court; and

Subsection.Text: If subsection 722(1) of this Act and subsection 42(1) of the other Act come into force on the same day, then that subsection 722(1) is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 42(1) and subsection (5) applies as a consequence.
On the first day on which both subsection 43(1) of the other Act and subsection 723(1) of this Act are in force, subsection 103(1) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

In addition to any right against the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund under section 101, a person may file a claim with the Administrator for the loss, damage, costs or expenses if the person has suffered loss or damage or incurred costs or expenses referred to in sections 51, 71, 74.24 and 77, Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention and Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention in respect of any kind of loss, damage, costs or expenses arising out of actual or anticipated oil pollution damage, including economic loss caused by oil pollution suffered by persons whose property has not been polluted but not including loss in respect of damage as defined in paragraph 6(a) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention.
On the first day on which both subsection 43(2) of the other Act and subsection 723(3) of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 103(3) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (1.1) do not apply to a response organization referred to in paragraph 51(1)(a), 71(1)(a), 74.24(1)(a) or 77(1)(b) or to a person in a state other than Canada.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 106.1(6) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a response organization referred to in paragraph 51(1)(a), 71(1)(a), 74.24(1)(a) or 77(1)(b) or to a person in a state other than Canada.
On the first day on which both section 31 of the other Act and section 725 of this Act are in force, paragraph 106.1(1)(b) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claim is the first one that the claimant has filed — other than one in relation to damage as defined in paragraph 6(a) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention — in respect of a given occurrence for loss, damage, costs or expenses referred to in paragraph (a) and does not exceed $35,000 or, if any other amount is fixed by regulations made under paragraph 106.8(b), that amount; and

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 45(1) of the other Act and section 725 of this Act are in force, paragraph 106.3(5)(a) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is precluded from pursuing any rights — except for economic loss referred to in subsection 103(1) — that they may have had, except in relation to damage as defined in paragraph 6(a) of Article 1 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention, against the Ship-source Oil Pollution Fund or any person in respect of matters referred to in sections 51, 71, 74.24 and 77, subsections 101(1.1) and 103(1.1), Article III of the Civil Liability Convention, Article 3 of the Bunkers Convention and Article 7 of the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention in relation to the occurrence to which the payment relates;

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 45(2) of the other Act and section 725 of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 106.3(5)(b) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Administrator is, to the extent of the payment to the claimant, subrogated to any of the claimant’s rights for any part of that payment that may be recovered from the owner of the ship, the International Fund, the Supplementary Fund, the HNS Fund or any other person liable.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 106.3(6) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Administrator directs a payment to be made under subsection (4), he or she shall take all reasonable measures to recover the part of the payment referred to in paragraph (5)(b) and, for that purpose, may commence an action in the Administrator’s or the claimant’s name, including a claim against the fund of the owner of a ship established under the Civil Liability Convention or the Hazardous and Noxious Substances Convention and may enforce any security provided to or enforceable by the claimant.
If section 728 of this Act comes into force before section 48 of the other Act, then that section 48 is repealed.
If section 728 of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 48 of the other Act, then that section 48 is deemed to have come into force before that section 728.

If subsection 50(5) of the other Act comes into force before subsection 734(1) of this Act, then, on the day on which that subsection 734(1) comes into force, subsection 117.2(4) of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

The Administrator is liable for any financial loss to the International Fund, the Supplementary Fund or the HNS Fund, as the case may be, as a result of a failure to communicate information under subsection (1) or (3).
If subsection 734(1) of this Act comes into force before subsection 50(5) of the other Act, then that subsection 50(5) is replaced by the following:
Subsection 117.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Administrator is liable for any financial loss to the International Fund, the Supplementary Fund or the HNS Fund, as the case may be, as a result of a failure to communicate information under subsection (1) or (3).

If subsection 734(1) of this Act comes into force on the same day as subsection 50(5) of the other Act, then that subsection 50(5) is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 734(1) and subsection (14) applies as a consequence.
On the first day on which both section 53 of the other Act and subsection 745(1) of this Act are in force, section 131 of the Marine Liability Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Every person or ship that contravenes subsection 55(1) or (2), 73(1) or (2), 74.28(1) or (2), 128(2) or 129(6) or (7) or regulations made under section 39 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

The provisions of this Division, other than sections 713 to 730, subsection 731(1), sections 732 and 733, subsections 734(1) and 735(1) and (3), sections 736 to 742, subsections 743(1), 744(1) and 745(1) and section 746, come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
